products.title: Bolbitis Difformis
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis,' also known as Mini Bolbitis or Davallia sp., is a captivating miniature aquatic fern ideal for adding natural elegance to your aquarium or terrarium. Originating from the Philippines, this fern is prized for its delicate, finely divided leaves that create a lush, carpet-like effect.

Appearance

This miniature fern features finely divided, delicate leaves that form a lush, carpet-like cover. Its unique texture and vibrant green color add a touch of natural beauty to any aquascape.

Care Requirements

  • Lighting: Thrives in low to moderate light conditions.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) and a pH range of 5.5-7.5.
  • Substrate: Can be attached to driftwood or rocks, or planted in nutrient-rich substrates.

Propagation

Propagates easily through rhizome division. Simply cut the rhizome and replant the segments to encourage new growth.

Benefits and Placement

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a lush, natural look to your aquarium or terrarium.
  • Versatility: Perfect for foreground placement or as an accent on hardscape elements.
  • Low Maintenance: Easy to care for, making it suitable for aquarists of all levels.

Conclusion

Enhance your aquarium with Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis.' Its delicate beauty, ease of care, and versatility make it a must-have for any aquascape. Order this stunning miniature fern today and transform your tank into a lush, green paradise.

Sold as bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root plants: Approximately 1-2 plants with loose bare roots.
  • Bunched plants: Approximately 1-2 plants bundled with foam and a plant weight.
  • Potted plants: Approximately 1-2 plants potted with rockwool and a net pot.

products.title: Helanthium Bolivianum Vitro Culture
products.body_html:

Sold as Tissue Culture plants in vitamin-rich gel | Bag size: Approx. 4 inches | Yields many small plants

Helanthium bolivianum, also known as Echinodorus bolivianum or the classic Chain Sword, is a vibrant and fast-spreading freshwater plant from South America. Known for its long, narrow, slightly curled leaves, this species is ideal for aquascapers looking to achieve dense, natural-looking foreground carpets or midground clusters.

Its bright green foliage creates a striking contrast in planted tanks and grows by sending out runners that rapidly form lush mats across the substrate. Grown using advanced Tissue Culture Technology, this plant arrives 100% free from algae, snails, and pathogens—ensuring a clean and healthy start in any aquarium setup.

products.title: Pogostemon Deccanensis Vitro Culture (Pogostemon Erectus)
products.body_html:
🌿 Pogostemon deccanensis Vitro Culture® – Indian Star Plant with Lush Needle-Like Foliage

Enhance your aquarium with the striking beauty of Pogostemon deccanensis, also known as the Indian Star plant. Formerly mislabeled in the hobby as Pogostemon erectus, this stunning species has since been correctly identified as Pogostemon deccanensis. Now cultivated using our Vitro Culture® method, it arrives completely pest-free, algae-free, and pesticide-free, ensuring a clean and healthy start for any aquascape.

This stem plant is known for its fine, needle-like foliage that resembles conifer branches, creating compact vertical clusters of vibrant green. Its lush, feathery texture adds motion and contrast when planted among broader-leaved or red-hued plants—making it a dynamic choice for both midground and background areas of the aquarium.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Pogostemon deccanensis

  • Synonym: Pogostemon erectus (obsolete)

  • Common Name: Indian Star Plant

  • Leaf Appearance: Fine, needle-like green foliage, dense and bushy

  • Growth Form: Upright vertical stems with compact foliage clusters

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Difficulty Level: Easy to Medium

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high for compact and healthy growth

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ beneficial but not strictly required; regular fertilization promotes optimal color and density

  • Growth Method: Submerged only

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates soft, textured clusters resembling miniature conifers

  • Adds vibrant light green contrast to darker or red plant species

  • Great for midground structure or soft background layering

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple healthy Pogostemon deccanensis plantlets—ready to rinse, acclimate, and plant in your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Newly introduced Pogostemon deccanensis may take time to adjust to submerged conditions. Yellowing or melt during early stages is common and will improve with stable conditions and light.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Never release Pogostemon deccanensis—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of unwanted portions responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: Bucephalandra White Marble
products.body_html:
🌿 Bucephalandra White Marble – Rare & Variegated Gem for High-Tech Aquascapes

Bucephalandra White Marble is one of the most visually stunning and challenging Bucephalandra varieties available. Known for its elegant marbled leaves featuring silvery-white and creamy variegation over a deep green to bluish canvas, this rare plant commands attention and admiration. Its refined beauty makes it a top-tier choice for aquascapers looking to elevate their layout with a sophisticated, high-contrast accent.

However, Buce White Marble is not for the faint of heart—this variety is hard to grow, requires stable high-tech conditions, and nutrient-rich substrate to maintain its delicate variegation and prevent melt. Best suited for experienced aquarists with advanced setups and CO₂ injection.

✨ Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. 'White Marble'

  • Common Name: Buce White Marble

  • Growth Form: Compact epiphyte with oval, variegated leaves

  • Leaf Color: Green to bluish base with marbled white streaks

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Required for healthy growth and stable variegation

  • Growth Rate: Very slow

  • Placement: Foreground, hardscape, or as a collectible midground accent

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature: 68–78°F (20–26°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to moderate

  • Lighting: Medium to strong, but avoid direct intense light to prevent bleaching

  • Tank Type: Best for high-tech aquariums with full macro and micro nutrient dosing

🖼 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Luxury Accent Plant: Adds elegance and contrast to driftwood or rockscape

  • Collector’s Choice: Rare and prized among advanced Buce enthusiasts

  • Textural Depth: Silky, marbled foliage creates depth and uniqueness in layout

  • Minimal Space Use: Compact size is ideal for nano tanks or focal points in larger aquascapes

🔁 Propagation & Growth:

  • Propagation: By carefully dividing the rhizome

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required – benefits from root contact with rich substrate or targeted root tabs

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – suitable for advanced terrarium or paludarium setups

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • Bare-root Plants: 1 premium bare-root Bucephalandra White Marble with at least 5 leaves

  • Potted Plants: 1 plant potted in rockwool and net pot for convenient planting

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Bucephalandra White Marble is an advanced plant that requires consistent CO₂, clean water, stable temperatures, and high-quality lighting. Its beautiful variegation can fade or melt under poor conditions. Not recommended for beginners. Always keep the rhizome above the substrate.

products.body_html:
Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus', also known as Echinodorus bolivianus 'Quadricostatus', is a popular aquatic plant among aquarium enthusiasts. It is a species of the Echinodorus genus and is native to Bolivia, Brazil, and Peru.

The plant is characterized by its long and narrow leaves that grow up to 20 inches in height and have a distinct reddish-brown coloration. Its leaves are arranged in a rosette pattern, and it forms runners that allow it to propagate and spread.

Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus' is a hardy plant that can thrive in a wide range of water conditions. It prefers moderate to high lighting and a nutrient-rich substrate. It can also tolerate a wide range of water hardness and pH levels.

In aquariums, Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus' is often used as a foreground or mid-ground plant, where its striking coloration and dense foliage can create a lush and vibrant underwater landscape. It also provides a natural habitat for small fish and invertebrates to hide and forage.

products.title: Christmas Moss Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Vesicularia montagnei, commonly known as Christmas Moss or Xmas Moss, is a beloved aquatic plant among aquarists for its unique, festive appearance and versatile growth habits. This moss features a slow growth rate and thrives best with the addition of CO2 and medium to high lighting conditions. When provided with these optimal conditions, Christmas Moss exhibits lush, green fronds that resemble the branches of a Christmas tree.

For best results, Christmas Moss should be attached to rocks, stones, or driftwood within the aquarium. Over time, it will develop roots and naturally adhere to these surfaces, creating a beautiful, natural aquascape. Like other types of moss, Christmas Moss adds a soft, textured look to the aquarium, making it a perfect choice for aquascaping.

In addition to its aesthetic appeal, Christmas Moss offers practical benefits for your tank's ecosystem. It provides hiding places and breeding grounds for small fish and shrimp, enhancing the biodiversity of your aquarium.

Ideal for both beginners and experienced hobbyists, Vesicularia montagnei (Christmas Moss) is a must-have for creating a vibrant, healthy, and visually appealing underwater environment. Whether you're designing a lush, green aquascape or seeking to add a touch of natural beauty to your tank, Christmas Moss is a perfect choice.

products.title: Pogostemon Deccanensis (Pogostemon Erectus)
products.body_html:
Pogostemon Deccanensis (formerly known as Pogostemon Erectus)

Pogostemon Deccanensis, also known as the Indian Star plant, a striking aquatic plant that stands out for its unique characteristics. This plant was known as Pogostemon Erectus for a long time but later correctly identified as Pogostemon Deccanensis. This versatile plant is perfect for adding texture and vibrant greenery to your aquarium.

Key Features:

  • Appearance: The light green, needle-like, narrow leaves grow densely on the stems, forming compact groups of conifer-like stems, 15-40 cm tall and 1-3 cm wide. This creates a dense, bushy appearance that adds depth and interest to your aquascape.
  • Color: Its bright green hue provides a refreshing contrast to darker or red-toned plants, making it a standout addition to any underwater landscape.
  • Growth Rate: With moderate growth and vigorous roots, this plant can flourish quickly under the right conditions, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists.
  • Light Requirements: Thrives under medium to high light (at least 0.5 W/litre), which helps the plant stay compact for a longer period, ensuring it maintains its vibrant color and healthy growth.
  • CO2 Requirements: While CO2 supplementation is beneficial, it is not mandatory. Enhanced growth and overall health can be achieved with CO2.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers soft to moderately hard water with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5, suitable for most tropical aquariums.
  • Temperature: Ideal for temperatures between 22-28°C (72-82°F), making it compatible with a wide range of aquarium setups.
  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cutting the stems and replanting the cuttings, allowing you to expand your plant collection effortlessly.

Placement and Care:

  • Background Plant: Pogostemon Deccanensis is suitable as a background plant and creates a wonderful focal point in both smaller and larger groups.
  • Frequent Pruning: The plant will need frequent pruning to maintain its compact shape. Cut-off shoots will easily start growing when planted.
  • Consistent Fertilization: Regular fertilization with macro and micronutrients supports healthy growth.
  • Stable Water Parameters: Maintain stable water parameters to avoid stress on the plant.
  • Emergent Growth: When cultivated emersed, Pogostemon Deccanensis forms beautiful candle-like flower spikes with a characteristic smell, provided it receives sufficient light.

Origin and History:

Pogostemon Deccanensis was originally collected for aquaristic purposes in the western Indian state of Maharashtra. Over the years, it has been used in many aquaria, mainly in the USA and increasingly in Europe. Its submersed growth habit is more upright and larger in dimensions compared to similar plants. The emersed form of Pogostemon Deccanensis further distinguishes it, with highly distinct, decorative flower spikes with violet flowers, unlike the inconspicuous flowers of other species. Additionally, Pogostemon Erectus has leaves reminiscent of pine needles with visible hairs on its stamina, a feature that sets it apart.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Schismatoglottis Prietoi Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Schismatoglottis Prietoi, an aquatic plant native to the Philippines, is a unique addition to the aquascaping world. It belongs to the family Araceae and is notable for its ability to thrive in fully aquatic environments, a rare trait within its genus. This small, colonial plant grows only up to 8 cm tall, featuring pale green to green, oblong to elliptical leaves with a smooth texture, sharply pointed tips, and broadly wavy edges.

Ideal for aquariums, Schismatoglottis Prietoi is known for its ease of care and adaptability to various water conditions. It prefers medium to low lighting and can flourish in temperatures ranging from 20 to 28°C. The plant's pH tolerance spans from 6.5 to 7.5, accommodating both soft and hard water types. While CO2 injection can promote faster growth, it's not a necessity for this plant.

Schismatoglottis Prietoi is particularly suited for smaller aquarium setups like Nano Cubes due to its compact growth pattern. It can be planted directly into the substrate or attached to hardscape materials like rocks and driftwood. This versatility, combined with its unique appearance and easy propagation through rhizomes, makes Schismatoglottis Prietoi a valuable plant for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

This species is a testament to the diversity and beauty of aquatic plants, offering a distinct look compared to more common aquarium plants like Anubias. It's a perfect choice for aquarists looking to create a natural, riverbed-like environment in their tanks.

When provided with soft water, bright lighting, and CO₂, Eriocaulon cinereum flourishes—sometimes producing slender flowering stalks that enhance its ornamental beauty. Limnophila sp. ‘Belem’ is a rare and elegant aquarium plant admired for its delicate textures and compact growth. Originally discovered near Belem, Brazil, this stem plant is distinguished by its long, narrow, serrated leaves that are naturally green but can shift into vibrant shades of red to deep purple depending on lighting intensity, CO₂ supplementation, and nutrient availability. Its fine-leafed structure makes it an excellent choice for adding contrast, color variety, and depth to aquascapes.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Limnophila sp. ‘Belem’

  • Common Name: Belem Needle Leaf

  • Origin: Brazil (Belem region)

  • Unique Foliage: Long, narrow, serrated leaves with vivid red coloration under high-tech conditions.

  • Compact Growth: Shorter and bushier than most Limnophila species, perfect for aquascaping.

  • Color Potential: Green foliage that transitions to reddish tones with high light and CO₂.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

products.title: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla, also known as Bucephalandra Kedagang Pink, is a captivating and rare aquatic plant that brings a burst of color and elegance to any aquarium. Renowned for its vibrant pink hues and unique foliage, this plant is highly sought after by aquarists looking to create a visually stunning and diverse aquascape. Ideal for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla is a must-have for any planted tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla

  1. Vibrant Pink Foliage: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla boasts striking pink leaves with shades that can range from light pink to deep magenta. This unique coloration adds a dramatic and eye-catching element to your aquarium, making it a focal point in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile Growth: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it perfect for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and texture to your tank.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Known for its resilience, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla thrives in a range of water conditions. It requires minimal maintenance and can adapt to both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups, making it suitable for all levels of aquarists.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its native environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The vibrant pink leaves of Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla create a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.


Sold as Bare-root Plants or Potted Plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 1-2 loose plants 
  • Potted Plants: Approximately 1-2 plants potted in rockwool and a net pot for secure growth and easy placement.

products.title: Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’
products.body_html:
Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’ – Floating Pennywort

Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’ is a stunning aquatic plant admired for its clover-like, scalloped leaves with vibrant green centers and creamy-white margins. The distinctive umbrella-shaped foliage floats gracefully on the water’s surface or creeps along edges, adding a fresh, luminous accent to ponds, aquariums, and container water gardens.

The variegated form offers a refined ornamental look, while its larger clover-shaped leaves resemble those of Hydrocotyle sibthorpioides but are noticeably broader and more striking. Whether floating freely or rooted in shallow margins, it instantly adds a natural, elegant touch to any aquascape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory®, each plant is carefully cultivated for optimal health and vibrant variegation, ensuring that it arrives ready to thrive and beautify your setup with its distinctive charm.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’

  • Growth Habit: Floating or creeping stems that root at nodes

  • Height: Low-growing, averaging around 6 inches tall

  • Native Range: North America (native aquatic species)

  • USDA Hardiness: Zones 5–11

  • Temperature: Hardy across USDA zones 5–11

  • Complements lilies, lotus, and other marginal aquatics

products.title: Micranthemum Monte Carlo
products.body_html:
🌿 Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo' – Lush, Fast-Spreading Carpet Plant

Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo', commonly known as Monte Carlo, is one of the most popular carpeting plants for planted aquariums. Its small, round, bright green leaves create a dense, vibrant carpet that brings a lush, natural look to any aquascape. Unlike some other carpeting species, Monte Carlo is relatively fast-growing and adaptable, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquascapers.

At Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, we cultivate Monte Carlo emersed for stronger growth and easier adaptation to your tank. When submerged with good light and CO₂, this plant will quickly spread across the substrate, creating a beautiful foreground or midground carpet.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo'

  • Common Names: Monte Carlo, MC, New Large Pearl Grass

  • Leaf Shape: Small, round

products.title: Glossostigma Elatinoides
products.body_html:
Glossostigma elatinoides, commonly referred to as 'Glosso' or 'small mud-mat'. It is a popular aquatic plant among aquarium enthusiasts, prized for its vibrant green color and carpeting growth habit. Originating from New Zealand, this delicate plant features small, rounded leaves and spreads quickly, forming a dense, lush carpet along the substrate of freshwater aquariums.

Characteristics:

Glossostigma elatinoides belongs to the Scrophulariaceae family and hails from New Zealand. It boasts bright green leaves and has a fast growth rate, typically reaching a height of 2-3 cm (0.8-1.2 inches). To thrive, it requires high to very high lighting and a substantial amount of CO2.

Care and Maintenance:

To maintain a healthy and thriving Glossostigma elatinoides carpet, it's essential to provide high-intensity lighting and consistent CO2 supplementation. This plant also benefits from a nutrient-rich substrate and regular fertilization. With proper care, Glosso will quickly cover the aquarium floor, providing an attractive, natural look.

Aquascaping Tips:

Glossostigma elatinoides is ideal for foreground placement, creating a seamless, green carpet effect. It is perfect for Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes, adding texture and visual interest. For best results, plant in small clusters, spacing them a few centimeters apart to encourage spreading. Regular trimming is necessary to prevent overgrowth and maintain a neat appearance.

Glossostigma elatinoides is a beautiful and versatile plant, making it a favorite choice for aquarists seeking to create stunning underwater landscapes.

products.title: Bucephalandra Rainbow Marble
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Rainbow Marble (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra which have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock or driftwood. 
  • Sold as a small loose, bare-root rhizome plant with 4+ leaves.

products.title: Rotala Indica (Ammannia Bonsai)
products.body_html:
Rotala Indica 'Bonsai,' also known as the true Rotala Indica, is often confused with Rotala Rotundifolia, a completely different species. Originally introduced as Ammannia sp. 'Bonsai,' its true identity was confirmed by Cavan Allen in 2009.

Distinctive Features: Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' is characterized by its thick, round leaves, with the tips turning a beautiful reddish hue under strong lighting. Unlike Rotala Rotundifolia, which tends to creep under intense light, Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' grows straight and upright, making it a striking addition to any aquascape.

Growth and Care: This plant is highly versatile, thriving in various aquarium settings. It is particularly popular in Street/Dutch-style aquariums and is ideal for small to nano tanks. Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' looks best when planted in small to medium groups, creating a lush and vibrant display.

Aquascaping Uses:

  • Dutch-Style Aquariums: Perfect for creating structured, layered layouts.
  • Nano Tanks: Its compact size makes it an excellent choice for smaller aquariums.
  • Group Planting: Grows beautifully in clusters, adding depth and color to your aquascape.

Enhance your aquarium with the unique beauty and growth habit of Rotala Indica 'Bonsai'. Shop now to add this true Rotala Indica to your collection and elevate your aquascaping designs.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

products.title: Utricularia Graminifolia
products.body_html:
Utricularia Graminifolia is celebrated as one of the most beautiful and popular foreground plants for planted aquariums. Its small size, fluffy appearance, and grass-like lawn effect make it a top choice for aquascaping, especially in nano tanks.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Utricularia Graminifolia
  • Common Name: UG, Grass-leaved Bladderwort
  • Family: Lentibulariaceae
  • Leaf Color: Bright green
  • Growth Form: Dense, carpet-like

Unique Characteristics

Utricularia Graminifolia stands out for its bright green, grass-like leaves that form a lush, dense carpet, creating a stunning visual effect in the foreground of any aquarium. Its compact size and fluffy texture make it ideal for intricate aquascape designs.

Ideal Conditions

To thrive, Utricularia Graminifolia requires specific conditions:

  • Light: High intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich soil
  • Water Temperature: 22-28°C (72-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for, given the right conditions:

  • Lighting: Ensure high light levels to promote healthy growth.
  • Anchoring: Due to its tiny root system, use stainless steel T-pins to anchor the plant to the substrate until it becomes established.
  • Dry Start Method (DSM): The DSM is highly effective for quickly establishing Utricularia Graminifolia, allowing it to access natural CO2 and grow rapidly.

Growth and Propagation

Once adapted to the environment, Utricularia Graminifolia develops a deep root system, spreading through runners to form a continuous carpet. The dry start method (DSM) is particularly effective, as it allows the plant to access an unlimited amount of natural CO2, boosting growth rates and ensuring a fully grown carpet in a short time.

Safety in Aquariums

Despite being a carnivorous plant, Utricularia Graminifolia is safe for aquarium inhabitants. Its tiny traps are designed to capture microscopic organisms and pose no threat to fish, shrimp, or other larger tank inhabitants. This makes it a unique and harmless addition to your aquascape.

Aquascaping Uses

Utricularia Graminifolia is versatile and ideal for various aquascaping applications:

  • Foreground Plant: Perfect for creating a dense, green carpet in the foreground.
  • Nano Tanks: Ideal for small tanks due to its compact and fluffy growth.
  • Carpet Plant: Its grass-like appearance makes it an excellent choice for lush carpeted areas.

The bright green, dense foliage of Utricularia Graminifolia provides excellent contrast and visual interest, enhancing the overall aesthetics of both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Utricularia Graminifolia is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a beautiful and unique foreground plant. Its vibrant green color, fluffy texture, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a stunning underwater landscape.

  • Sold as a small loose bare-root portion
  • Approx. 2" x 2" in size

products.title: Bucephalandra Velvet Tricolor
products.body_html:
🌿 Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor" – Rare Bushy Epiphyte

Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor" (sometimes referred to as Velvet 3 Color) is a rare, small, and densely bushy Bucephalandra form with narrow, wavy leaves. Believed to originate from the Sanggau area in West Kalimantan, Borneo, its precise species classification and exact locality remain uncertain. Despite its rarity, this plant has proven to be highly suitable for aquariums, with excellent growth habits and steady propagation.

Young leaves emerge in shades of light brown-red, later maturing into deep dark brown-green, creating a striking multicolor effect that inspires its name. Its compact, ramified rhizome structure forms a bushy cluster, making it a unique addition to any aquascape.

  • Coloration: Young leaves light brown-red, mature leaves dark brown-green

Originating from the crystal-clear streams of Borneo, this enchanting plant displays a soft metallic glow that shimmers beautifully under aquarium lighting—shifting between shades of pink, copper, and subtle violet.   • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional but beneficial for richer colors and faster growth.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; attach gently to hardscape using thread or aquarium-safe glue.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required—absorbs nutrients directly from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – an excellent choice for paludariums and humid terrarium displays.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds soft pink and copper tones that pair beautifully with green and dark-leaved Buce varieties.

  • Perfect for accenting focal points and enhancing contrast in planted aquariums.

  • Ideal for collector tanks, nano aquascapes, or refined color layering in nature layouts.

  • Creates a gentle, luxurious aesthetic that enhances the overall harmony of an aquascape.

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, all of our Bucephalandra species are 100% farm-raised—never taken from the wild. Each plant originates ex-vitro (grown from sterile lab culture) before being transitioned to hydroponic growth in our controlled environment. This ensures exceptional quality, pest-free plants, and sustainable preservation of Borneo’s native ecosystems.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose Bare-Root or Potted plants:

  • 1 Bare-Root: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Bare-Root: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 1 Potted: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Red Root Floater
products.body_html:
Red Root Floater (Phyllanthus fluitans) are a striking and vibrant addition to any freshwater aquarium or pond. Known for their beautiful red roots and green leaves that turn a stunning reddish hue under optimal conditions, these floating plants add a unique and eye-catching element to aquatic landscapes.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Coloration: Features green leaves that develop a reddish tint and striking red roots, enhancing the visual appeal of any water feature.
  • Floating Plant: As a floating plant, Red Root Floaters do not need to be planted in substrate, making them easy to add to any setup.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under moderate to high lighting conditions, which promotes the red coloration of the leaves and roots.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 70°F to 82°F (21°C to 28°C).

Aquarium and Pond Benefits:

  • Shade and Shelter: Provides shade and shelter for fish and other aquatic inhabitants, helping to reduce stress and create a more natural environment.
  • Nutrient Absorption: Helps absorb excess nutrients from the water, reducing algae growth and improving water quality.
  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a unique and colorful element to the water surface, enhancing the overall aesthetic of aquariums and ponds.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy Care: Requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.
  • Propagation: Propagates easily through division, allowing for quick and easy expansion of plant coverage.

Conclusion:

Red Root Floater (Phyllanthus fluitans) are an excellent choice for aquarists and pond keepers looking to add a splash of color and natural beauty to their water features. Their vibrant red roots and colorful leaves make them a standout addition to any aquatic environment. Easy to care for and highly beneficial for water quality, Red Root Floaters are perfect for creating a healthy and visually stunning aquatic landscape.

Enhance your aquarium or pond with the vibrant beauty and natural benefits of Red Root Floaters and enjoy the dynamic, colorful touch they bring to your aquatic setup.

products.body_html:
🌺 Nymphaea Micrantha

Rare Tri-Color Dwarf Lotus • Aquarium Water Lily

Nymphaea micrantha is a beautiful and rare dwarf lotus prized for its unique tri-color foliage, displaying shades of green, bronze, red, and purple depending on lighting and nutrients. Native to tropical West Africa, this compact water lily makes an excellent centerpiece plant for planted aquariums without overwhelming smaller tanks.

With proper care, Nymphaea micrantha produces elegant submerged leaves and may send surface leaves if allowed, creating a natural and dynamic aquascape.

  • Placement: Mid-ground to Background

    (can grow taller if floating leaves are not trimmed)

products.title: Anubias Petite White Platinum
products.body_html:
Anubias Petite White Platinum – Rare Elegance for Premium Aquascapes

Anubias Petite White Platinum is a breathtaking and extremely rare variety of Anubias barteri var. nana ‘Petite’, known for its delicate, albino white leaves and compact growth habit. Perfect for nano aquariums, high-end aquascapes, and rare plant collectors, this exquisite variety offers stunning visual contrast but requires special care due to its low chlorophyll content. Its rarity, slow growth, and pure platinum appearance make it a prized centerpiece for serious aquarists.

Key Features, Benefits, and Growing Tips:

🌿 Albino Platinum-White Leaves:
Features brilliant, white-toned foliage caused by a lack of green pigment (chlorophyll), creating a luminous, elegant appearance.

💧 Extremely Slow-Growing:
Maintains a miniature size with leaves around 0.5–1 inch, ideal for nano aquariums and intricate hardscape designs.

🪨 Attach to Hardscape:
Grows best when securely attached to driftwood, rocks, or decorations. Never bury the rhizome to prevent rot.

🔆 High Care Requirements:
Due to the lack of chlorophyll, this plant struggles with photosynthesis and must be placed in low-light, shaded areas to prevent burning and decline.

🌱 Propagation Through Rhizome Division:
Slowly propagates by carefully dividing healthy sections of the rhizome.

🐠 Perfect For:
Rare plant collectors, luxury nano aquariums, shaded aquascapes, and experienced hobbyists seeking a truly unique aquatic specimen.

Why Choose Anubias Petite White Platinum for Your Aquarium?

Aquascapers seeking a rare, show-stopping aquatic plant that offers refined beauty will find Anubias Petite White Platinum an unmatched choice. Its shimmering white foliage brings a sense of purity and luxury to underwater landscapes. However, due to its lack of green pigment, it requires experienced care: protection from intense lighting, careful placement in shaded areas, and stable, clean water parameters.

Unlike typical green plants that easily photosynthesize, the White Platinum Petite needs extra attention to prevent light stress and maintain its health. For advanced aquarists and collectors, successfully growing this plant is both a rewarding challenge and a bold statement of skill and dedication.

If you’re looking to elevate your aquascape to a truly elite level, Anubias White Platinum Petite is the perfect addition.

Product Specifications:

  • Scientific Name: Anubias barteri var. nana 'Petite White Platinum'

  • Common Name: Anubias Petite White Platinum

  • Position: Foreground, Hardscape Attachment

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not necessary (but stable CO₂ helps health)

  • Growth Rate: Very Slow

  • Difficulty: Very Hard

  • Leaf Size: Very Small (~0.5–1 inch)

  • Sold as a small, nano-size bare-root plant.

products.title: Rotala Macrandra Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Rotala Macrandra is a magnificent aquarium plant, however, it is quite demanding. This variety is a little simpler to grow and produces vibrant colors. It requires a lot of light to produce its gorgeous pink and red colors. It also preferred soft water and lots of CO2, micro, and macronutrients are essential for healthy growth.

Farm-raised Rotala Macrandra is easily broken in transit because of the fragile stems. It is also harder to adapt to the new environment when the plant reaches the mature stage. Therefore, here at Aquarium Plants Factory, we always recommended buying Rotala Macrandra in vitro, tissue culture version because they are still a young plant, sterile, and easier to adapt to your aquarium compare to the adult, farm raise version. Not to mention you get many plants in each cup, no snails, no algae, and perfect for any aquascape design.

products.title: Echinodorus Gabrielii
products.body_html:
Echinodorus Gabrielii is a rare, exotic plant that is a great addition to any aquarium. This compact, Gabriel's Sword plant stays short and lush when it grows underwater, in the aquarium.

products.title: Ceratopteris Siliquosa Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Ceratopteris Siliquosa, commonly known as Water Sprite, is a highly versatile and fast-growing aquatic plant that can transform any aquarium into a lush, green oasis. Known for its delicate, fern-like leaves and easy care requirements, Water Sprite is an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. This plant can be used as a floating plant, a midground anchor, or as a background plant, offering flexibility in aquascaping designs.

Key Features of Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  1. Delicate, Fern-Like Leaves: Water Sprite boasts finely divided, lace-like leaves that create a beautiful and airy appearance. The light green foliage adds a refreshing and natural look to any aquarium, making it an attractive focal point.

  2. Versatile Growth Options: This plant can be grown either floating on the water surface or anchored in the substrate. Its adaptability allows for creative aquascaping, whether you want a floating canopy or a rooted background plant.

  3. Fast Growth Rate: Ceratopteris Siliquosa is known for its rapid growth, helping to quickly fill in empty spaces in your tank. This fast growth rate also makes it effective at outcompeting algae for nutrients, contributing to a cleaner and healthier aquarium environment.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to tropical regions around the world, Water Sprite thrives in warm, freshwater environments. It naturally grows in shallow waters, making it an ideal choice for replicating tropical biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: Propagating Water Sprite is simple and straightforward. New plantlets form along the leaves and can be separated and replanted. This makes it easy to expand your collection and maintain a lush, green aquarium.

Care Tips for Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting is ideal for Water Sprite. Adequate light supports its rapid growth and helps maintain its vibrant green color.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-82°F (20-28°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in a variety of water conditions, making it highly adaptable.
  • Substrate: While Water Sprite can be grown floating, it can also be anchored in the substrate. A nutrient-rich substrate will support its growth, but it can also absorb nutrients directly from the water.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will promote healthy growth and vibrant foliage. CO2 supplementation is not strictly necessary but can enhance its development.
  • Water Flow: Moderate water flow is ideal for keeping the leaves of Water Sprite clean and free from debris, mimicking its natural environment.

Benefits of Adding Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The delicate, fern-like leaves of Water Sprite add a stunning visual effect, enhancing the natural beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides excellent cover and hiding spots for fish and invertebrates, creating a more dynamic and balanced ecosystem.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Water Sprite helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water, reducing the risk of algae outbreaks.

Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance, fast growth, and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Bacopa Platinum Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Bacopa Monnieri Platinum is a unique version of the popular Bacopa Monnieri plant used in aquariums. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we're among the first to offer this special type of plant. The original Bacopa Monnieri, also called Moneywort or Water Hyssop, is easy to look after and grows fast in many settings. But this new version, the Platinum, is not common. It's harder to take care of because it doesn't have the usual green color that helps it photosensitive from sunlight.

You can put Bacopa Monnieri Platinum in the front part of your aquarium, but you need to look after it by trimming and taking care of it. When there's not a lot of light, it might get long and thin as it reaches for the light. But if you give it strong light and good nutrients, it will stay short and the stems will spread out really close to the bottom of the tank, like a creeping plant. Just remember, this special plant needs a bit more attention than the regular green one.

Hygrophila Pinnatifida is a unique and highly decorative aquarium plant originating from the Western Ghats of India. Loved for its deeply lobed leaves, bronze-to-reddish coloration, and distinctive horizontal growth habit, this species adds bold texture and contrast to planted aquariums. It naturally attaches to hardscape like rocks and driftwood, making it a versatile and visually captivating choice for aquascaping.

This listing features our Vitro Culture version, cultivated in sterile conditions and carefully sealed in a 4-inch bag. Ideal for advanced aquascapers and shrimp-safe environments, these lab-grown plants are 100% free from algae, pests, and pesticides.

  • Placement: Midground or hardscape attachment

  • Growth Rate: Medium

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature Range: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

products.title: Java Fern Large Size
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus – Large Size Java Fern for Instant Impact

Microsorum pteropus, commonly known as Java Fern, is one of the most beloved and beginner-friendly aquatic plants in the hobby. This special large size variant, standing approximately 8–10 inches tall, is ideal for aquarists seeking a more mature, ready-to-display plant to add instant structure and greenery to their aquascape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate these large-size Java Ferns in emersed conditions, resulting in strong, vibrant leaves and thick rhizomes that transition beautifully when submerged. The classic, sword-like leaves are rich green in color and fan outward in a graceful arch, making them perfect for midground to background placement in medium to large aquariums.

Java Fern is a rhizome-based plant and should always be attached to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape. Never bury the rhizome in substrate, as this may lead to rot. It is incredibly hardy, low maintenance, and adaptable to a wide range of water parameters—making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Special Large Size: Grows approx. 8–10" tall—perfect for instant visual impact

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Tolerates various lighting and water conditions

  • Attachable Rhizome: Secure to wood or stone—do not bury

  • Low-Tech Friendly: Grows without CO₂ and requires minimal fertilization

  • Classic Aquascaping Plant: A staple for nature-style and traditional aquascapes

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus

  • Common Names: Java Fern

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Midground / Background (Attach to hardscape)

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

  • Origin: Southeast Asia

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Low to medium light preferred

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: CO₂ not required; occasional fertilization supports long-term health

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation Method: Rhizome division

products.title: Helanthium Tenellum (Dwarf Chain Sword)
products.body_html:
Helanthium Tenellum, previously known as Echinodorus Tenellus, is an excellent foreground plant for fish tanks and aquariums. This member of the chain sword group has been reclassified into the genus Helanthium, but it remains a popular choice among aquarists for its aesthetic appeal and ease of care.

Key Features:

  • Grass-Like Appearance: Helanthium Tenellum, commonly referred to as Dwarf Chain Sword or Pygmy Chain Sword, features compact, narrow leaves that resemble grass, making it an ideal foreground plant for creating a lush, green carpet in your aquarium.
  • Color Transformation: Under strong light conditions, the leaves of Helanthium Tenellum turn a beautiful reddish-green, adding a dynamic and colorful element to your aquascape.
  • Easy Propagation: This plant propagates easily through runners, allowing it to spread quickly and form a dense carpet that enhances the visual appeal of any tank.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The grass-like, compact leaves of Helanthium Tenellum create a beautiful and natural-looking carpet in the foreground of your aquarium, providing a visually pleasing environment for both you and your aquatic inhabitants.
  • Low Maintenance: Helanthium Tenellum is a hardy plant that thrives in various water conditions. Its ability to propagate through runners makes it easy to maintain and expand within your tank.
  • Versatile: Suitable for both small and large aquariums, this plant can adapt to different light conditions, making it a versatile choice for aquascapers.

Ideal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under strong light conditions, which help enhance its reddish-green coloration.
  • Water Parameters: Adaptable to a wide range of water conditions, making it easy to care for in different aquarium setups.
  • Substrate: Requires a nutrient-rich substrate to support healthy growth and propagation.

Why Choose Helanthium Tenellum?

Helanthium Tenellum is perfect for aquarists looking to create a lush, green carpet in their aquarium. Its compact, grass-like leaves, ease of care, and ability to propagate quickly make it an ideal foreground plant. Whether you are setting up a new tank or enhancing an existing one, Helanthium Tenellum will add a touch of natural beauty and elegance to your underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquarium with the beautiful and easy-to-care-for Helanthium Tenellum. Enjoy the lush, green carpet it creates and the vibrant colors it brings to your underwater world.

products.title: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is a rare and captivating aquatic plant that adds a unique touch of elegance to any aquarium. Known for its velvety, dark green leaves and easy care requirements, this plant is a favorite among aquarists looking to enhance their aquascapes with a distinctive and eye-catching element. Perfect for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is a must-have for any planted tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong

  1. Velvety Dark Green Leaves: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong boasts rich, velvety leaves that display a deep green hue, sometimes with subtle iridescent highlights. This unique texture and coloration make it a standout feature in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and structure to your aquarium.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is known for its resilience and adaptability. It thrives in a range of water conditions and requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. This natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its natural environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The velvety, dark green leaves of Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong add a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Drosera Rotundifolia Vitro Culture (Round-leaved Sundew)
products.body_html:
Drosera rotundifolia, commonly known as the round-leaved sundew, is a fascinating carnivorous plant native to boggy, acidic environments across the Northern Hemisphere, including parts of Europe, Asia, and North America. This perennial plant features distinctive, circular leaves covered in glandular hairs that exude a sticky, dew-like substance. These glistening droplets attract and trap unsuspecting insects, which are then digested by the plant to supplement its nutrient intake, compensating for the poor soil conditions in its natural habitat.

The leaves of Drosera rotundifolia are typically a vibrant green with green or red-tipped tentacles, providing a striking contrast. These tentacles coil around captured prey, enhancing the plant's ability to secure its meal. The plant's rosette growth form allows it to spread out and maximize its trapping efficiency.

During the summer, Drosera rotundifolia produces delicate, white or pink flowers on slender stems that rise above the foliage, adding to its unique beauty. Each flower opens for just a single day, usually in succession, creating a subtle yet charming display over several weeks. The flowers are self-pollinating, ensuring seed production even in isolated conditions.

Tips

  • Feeding: Do not fertilize. The plant gets its nutrients from the insects it traps. If grown indoors, occasional feeding with small insects can be beneficial.
  • Dormancy: In winter, Drosera rotundifolia enters a dormancy period. Reduce watering slightly but ensure the soil remains damp. Keep it in a cooler location during this time.
  • Pests and Diseases: Generally pest-free, but watch out for aphids or fungus gnats. Overwatering or poor ventilation can lead to fungal infections.

Propagation

  • Seeds: Collect seeds from flowers and sow them on the surface of a moist sphagnum moss and sand mix. Ensure high humidity and bright light for germination.
  • Leaf Cuttings: Cut a healthy leaf with some stem and place it on moist sphagnum moss. Cover with a plastic bag to maintain humidity. New plantlets will form at the base of the leaf.
  • Division: During repotting, carefully divide the rosettes and replant them separately.

Drosera rotundifolia thrives in nutrient-poor, waterlogged soils and prefers full sun to partial shade. It is well-suited to growing in containers with a mix of sphagnum moss and sand, replicating its natural bog environment. This sundew is an excellent choice for enthusiasts of unusual plants, offering both a captivating appearance and a fascinating glimpse into the world of carnivorous flora.

Ideal for wetland gardens, terrariums, or as a unique houseplant, Drosera rotundifolia requires consistent moisture and humidity to thrive. It is a low-maintenance plant, provided its growing conditions are met, and can live for many years, forming dense, attractive mats of insect-catching foliage.

  • Sold as a Tissue Culture plants with vitamin liquid/gel
  • Bag size: Approx. 4 inches. Yield 2-3 plants
  • Sustainable growth aquarium plants species
  • Tissue Culture Technology | 100% Free from Snail, Algae, Pathogen, and Bacteria plus Extended Shelf Life on all in Vitro Plants

Scientific Name

 Drosera Rotundifolia

Common Name

 Round-leaved Sundew, Roundleaf Sundew, or Common Sundew
Type Rosette

Size

 Generally small, with rosettes reaching about 2 to 4 inches (5 to 10 cm) in diameter

Temperature

 Prefers cool to moderate temperatures. Can tolerate slight frost, but extreme cold should be avoided

Native Habitat

 Boggy, acidic environments across the Northern Hemisphere, including Europe, Asia, and North America

Flower

 Delicate white or pink, borne on slender stems, blooming in succession during summer

Water

 Keep the soil consistently moist. Use rainwater, distilled water, or deionized water to prevent mineral buildup. Avoid tap water as it may contain minerals harmful to the plant

Humidity

 Thrives in high humidity environments. Consider using a humidity tray or growing in a terrarium if the ambient humidity is low

Soil

 Requires nutrient-poor, waterlogged soils. Ideal mix is a combination of sphagnum moss and sand to mimic its natural bog environment

Lighting Requirements

 Prefers full sun to partial shade. Ensure it receives at least 4-6 hours of direct sunlight daily

products.title: Eriocaulon Vietnam
products.body_html:
Vietnam Eriocaulon (Eriocaulon sp. Vietnam) is a rare and highly sought-after aquarium plant, originating from Thanh Hoa, Vietnam. Known for its grass-like appearance and broad, arching medium-green leaves, this species reaches a height of 15–30 cm, making it a beautiful midground or background choice in aquascapes. Often compared to Eriocaulon sp. "Goias" due to its similar structure, Vietnam Eriocaulon stands out with its ease of care and adaptability.

Unlike the more challenging Blyxa japonica, Vietnam Eriocaulon is easier to cultivate and requires less attention, thriving in a variety of water conditions. It grows well in moderate lighting and can even flourish in hard water environments, making it more versatile than other Eriocaulon species. While it appreciates nutrient-rich substrates and CO2 supplementation, it requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both intermediate and experienced aquarists.

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Appearance: Grass-like plant with medium-green, arching leaves, creating a striking focal point in any aquarium.
  • Compact and Versatile: Grows to a height of 15–30 cm, suitable for midground or background placement in aquascapes.
  • Easy to Grow: Simpler to cultivate than other species, including Blyxa japonica, making it more accessible to a wider range of hobbyists.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under moderate lighting, though it can adapt to slightly higher levels for optimal growth.
  • Water Parameters: Tolerates a wide range of water conditions, including hard water, with a pH between 6.0 and 7.5 and temperatures of 68°F to 78°F (20°C to 26°C).
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Benefits from a nutrient-rich environment to support healthy growth and vibrant foliage.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Low Maintenance: Requires minimal upkeep, making it an excellent choice for aquarists looking for a beautiful yet easy-to-manage plant.
  • Aquascaping Flexibility: Works well as a midground or background plant in a variety of setups, enhancing both nano and larger aquariums.
  • Hard Water Tolerance: Unlike many other Eriocaulon species, Vietnam Eriocaulon thrives in hard water, making it an adaptable option for different aquarium environments.

Conclusion:

Vietnam Eriocaulon is a unique and rare plant that offers the beauty of grass-like foliage with the ease of low maintenance. Ideal for aquarists seeking a striking yet manageable plant, its adaptability to moderate light and hard water conditions makes it a standout addition to both beginner and advanced planted tanks. Elevate your aquascape with the natural elegance of Vietnam Eriocaulon, a plant that offers beauty without the hassle.

products.title: Littorella Uniflora (American Micro Sword)
products.body_html:
Littorella Uniflora, commonly known as American Micro Sword, US Micro Sword, American Shoregrass, or American Shoreweed, is a versatile and attractive plant native to the United States. This grass-like plant is perfect for terrariums, ponds, and aquariums, making it a popular choice among aquarists and hobbyists.

Key Features:

  • Grass-like Appearance: Resembles the well-known Lilaeopsis in appearance, adding a natural, green touch to your aquatic or terrestrial setups.
  • Versatile Growth: Suitable for terrariums, ponds, and aquariums, providing flexibility in various environments.
  • Easy Propagation: Propagates easily via runners, allowing it to develop a full lawn carpet over time.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Planting: Each rosette can be planted a few millimeters apart to encourage spreading and growth.
  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which help maintain its vibrant green color.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 68°F to 78°F (20°C to 26°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Ideal for Carpets: Excellent for creating lush carpet effects in the foreground of fish tanks and aquariums.
  • Natural Aesthetic: Adds a natural and appealing look to your aquatic environment, enhancing the overall visual appeal.
  • Foreground Placement: Perfect for foreground planting, providing a soft, green lawn-like appearance.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Regular Trimming: Trim regularly to maintain a neat and uniform carpet.
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Grows well in nutrient-rich substrates that provide essential nutrients for healthy growth.
  • Moderate Maintenance: Requires moderate care, making it suitable for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

Conclusion:

Littorella Uniflora (American Micro Sword, US Micro Sword, American Shoregrass, American Shoreweed) is an excellent choice for aquarists and hobbyists looking to create a lush, green carpet in their aquariums, terrariums, or ponds. Its grass-like appearance and easy propagation make it a standout addition to any setup. By providing the right conditions and care, you can enjoy the natural beauty and versatility of Littorella Uniflora in your aquatic or terrestrial environments.

Enhance your aquarium, terrarium, or pond with the natural charm of Littorella Uniflora and enjoy the lush, green landscape it brings to your space.

products.title: Aponogeton Madagascariensis (Madagascar Lace)
products.body_html:
Aponogeton madagascariensis – The Iconic Madagascar Lace Plant for Display Aquascapes

Aponogeton madagascariensis, famously known as the Madagascar Lace Plant, is one of the most visually striking and sought-after aquarium plants in the hobby. Its uniquely patterned, lattice-like leaves resemble delicate green lace, offering an extraordinary centerpiece for planted tanks. Native to slow-moving rivers and lakes in Madagascar, this elegant aquatic bulb plant thrives in cooler, well-oxygenated water and rewards aquarists with graceful growth and unrivaled texture.

This plant is ideal for display tanks or Dutch-style aquascapes where focal points are essential. With the right care and attention, Aponogeton madagascariensis can become the crown jewel of your aquarium.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Aponogeton madagascariensis

  • Common Name: Madagascar Lace Plant

  • Growth Form: Bulb plant with large, netted, lace-like leaves

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional, but improves growth and leaf size

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Placement: Midground to background centerpiece

  • Leaf Color: Bright to dark green, depending on light and nutrients

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature: 64°F to 75°F (18°C to 24°C) — prefers cooler water

  • pH Range: 6.0 to 7.5

  • Water Flow: Gentle to moderate flow; avoid stagnant conditions

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate or root tabs beneficial

  • Lighting: Bright indirect light; avoid algae-inducing extremes

✨ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds a dramatic and artistic touch with its intricate leaf pattern

  • Excellent for biotope or showcase tanks

  • Creates gentle movement and contrast in layout design

  • Rare, exotic species that impresses aquascaping enthusiasts

🌿 Propagation & Growth:

  • Propagation: Grown from a bulb; occasionally produces seeds

  • Dormancy: May enter a dormant phase—do not discard the bulb

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended for long-term health

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No, this species is strictly aquatic

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as Bare-root Plants, Sprouts Bulb With Leaf, or Bulb Without Leaf:

  • Bare-root Plants - A healthy loose specimen without bulb

  • Sprouted Bulb with Leaf – A healthy bulb with at least two lace leaf already growing

  • Bulb Only (No Leaf) – A firm, viable bulb without current foliage (ideal for patient aquarists)

Important Note: Bulbs sold without leaves are viable and may sprout within a few weeks under the right conditions. However, successful growth depends on proper care, stable water parameters, and some level of experience. As sprouting is not guaranteed, we strongly recommend selecting the Sprouted Bulb with Leaf if you prefer a more reliable and hassle-free start.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

This is a sensitive and specialty plant not suited for beginners. Water quality, cool temperature, and stable parameters are key to long-term success. Bulbs may take time to adjust after shipping—please plant with care and patience.

products.title: Microsorum Pteropus 'Needle'
products.body_html:
Microsorum Pteropus 'Needle' is a unique and attractive cultivar of the popular aquatic plant, Java Fern. As its name suggests, this plant is characterized by its long, narrow, and pointed leaves, which can grow up to 25 cm (10 inches) in length under optimal conditions. The plant is a slow-grower, but can reach a height of up to 20 cm (8 inches) with patience.

This low-maintenance plant is well-suited for both novice and experienced aquarists. It is adaptable to a wide range of water conditions, including slightly acidic to slightly alkaline pH levels and soft to moderately hard water. The plant can thrive in low to moderate light and does not require CO2 supplementation. However, regular fertilization is recommended to promote healthy growth and maintain its vibrant coloration.

One of the benefits of Microsorum Pteropus 'Needle' is that it is easy to care for and can be attached to driftwood or rocks in an aquarium using fishing line or thread until it establishes a strong hold. It is important not to bury the rhizome (horizontal stem) of the plant, as this can cause it to rot.

The 'Needle' variety of Java Fern is known for its striking and unique appearance, which can add a touch of texture and depth to any aquascape. The plant is suitable for midground or background placement in an aquarium and can be used to create a natural-looking aquatic environment.

In summary, Microsorum Pteropus 'Needle' is a beautiful and low-maintenance aquatic plant that can add a unique and eye-catching element to any aquarium. It is a great choice for aquarists who want to add a touch of texture and depth to their aquascape without too much effort.

  • Loose rhizome | approx. 6 leaf, loose, bare-root, small portion
  • Potted plants | approx. 6 leaf, potted plants with rockwool and net pot

products.title: Dwarf Water Lettuce
products.body_html:
🌿 Dwarf Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes 'Dwarf') | Floating Aquarium & Pond Plant

Dwarf Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes 'Dwarf') is a compact, ornamental floating plant perfect for aquariums, nano tanks, and small ponds. Its petite, velvety leaves form rosettes that float gracefully on the water's surface, creating a soft, natural look while providing shade and shelter for aquatic life. The smaller size makes it easier to manage than standard Water Lettuce, especially in indoor aquariums or tight spaces.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Compact Floating Plant: Ideal for aquariums, nano tanks, and small outdoor ponds.

  • Natural Water Purification: Absorbs excess nutrients, helping reduce algae growth.

  • Attractive Rosettes: Soft green, lettuce-like leaves with delicate floating roots.

  • Aquatic Habitat Enhancement: Offers shade and hiding spots for fish, fry, and shrimp.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Moderate to high; thrives in bright conditions with calm water.

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; temperature 70–85°F (21–29°C)

  • Placement: Floating on the surface; no planting required.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Enhances the natural appearance of aquariums and ponds.

  • Provides gentle surface coverage to reduce light penetration and algae.

  • Supports a healthy ecosystem by offering shade and protection for aquatic life.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Dwarf Water Lettuce is a sensitive tropical species. It cannot survive freezing temperatures outdoors and may require careful seasonal management. Always monitor growth to prevent overpopulation in ponds.

products.title: Bucephalandra Achilles Black
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Achilles Black (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.

products.title: Mayaca Fluviatilis
products.body_html:
Mayaca fluviatilis – Stream Bogmoss

Mayaca fluviatilis, also known as Stream Bogmoss, is a delicate, fast-growing stem plant treasured for its soft, feathery appearance and vibrant green coloration. Native to the slow-moving waters of Central and South America, this aquatic beauty brings a natural, flowing texture to freshwater aquariums. Its thin, grass-like leaves grow densely along slender stems, creating an elegant, mossy effect that adds depth and movement to any aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Feathery, Flowing Texture: Thin, needle-like leaves grow in dense whorls along the stem, mimicking the look of soft moss or grass.

  • Vibrant Green Coloration: Stays lush green in moderate light but becomes even brighter with high lighting and CO₂.

  • Rapid Growth: Fast-growing under ideal conditions, making it great for filling space or creating lush plant backdrops.

  • Versatile Placement: Suitable for midground to background in planted aquariums, or as a soft accent in nature-style layouts.

Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Performs well under moderate to high light. Stronger lighting encourages more compact, vibrant growth.

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: CO₂ supplementation and regular fertilization will enhance color and speed up growth, but it can adapt to low-tech setups.

  • Water Parameters: pH 5.5–7.5 | Temperature 72–82°F (22–28°C)

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Look: Creates a soft, natural aesthetic that complements other fine-leaved plants and hardscape elements.

  • Shelter for Aquatic Life: Offers hiding spaces for shrimp, fry, and small fish due to its bushy, dense growth.

  • Water Quality Support: Absorbs excess nutrients and helps compete with algae in well-maintained aquariums.

Care & Propagation Tips:

  • Trimming: Regular trimming is recommended to prevent the lower stems from becoming sparse and to encourage bushier growth.

  • Propagation: Propagates easily—cut stems and replant directly into the substrate.

  • Tank Mates: Compatible with a wide range of freshwater fish and invertebrates, including tetras, rasboras, and shrimp.

Conclusion:

Mayaca fluviatilis is an ideal plant for aquarists seeking a soft, flowing texture with rich green tones. Whether you’re designing a jungle-style aquascape or looking to add movement to your midground, this fast-growing stem plant provides beauty, versatility, and functionality. A favorite among hobbyists for its adaptability and graceful form.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Water Lettuce
products.body_html:
🌿 Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) | Floating Aquarium & Pond Plant

Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) is a classic floating plant known for its beautiful, rosette-shaped leaves that resemble small heads of lettuce. Its soft, velvety foliage and long dangling roots provide excellent cover and natural filtration for ponds and aquariums. Water Lettuce thrives in calm, nutrient-rich waters, helping to shade your aquatic environment and reduce algae growth.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Natural Filtration: Absorbs excess nutrients, improving water quality.

  • Floating Growth: Perfect for ponds, water gardens, and outdoor tubs.

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Velvety green leaves form charming rosettes atop the water surface.

  • Fish & Wildlife Benefits: Provides shade and protection for fish, frogs, and other aquatic life.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; prefers full sun in outdoor ponds.

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; temperature 70–85°F (21–29°C)

  • Placement: Floating on the water surface; no planting needed.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Reduces algae by blocking light and absorbing excess nutrients.

  • Creates a natural, lush surface for ponds and outdoor water features.

  • Provides refuge for fry, shrimp, and aquatic critters.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as a small portion of loose,  Lettuce rosettes (approximately 1–2 plants, depending on size and season).

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Water Lettuce is sensitive to frost and should not be used in outdoor settings during freezing temperatures. In tropical and subtropical climates, this plant can spread rapidly. Always monitor and maintain responsibly.

products.title: Bucephalandra Red Catherine
products.body_html:
🌸 Bucephalandra ‘Red Catherine’ – Rare Scarlet Jewel of Borneo

Bucephalandra ‘Red Catherine’, also known as Buce Red Catherine, is a breathtaking and rare Bucephalandra variety admired for its deep red-to-bronze foliage and graceful, elongated leaves. Native to the lush, fast-flowing rivers of Borneo, this exquisite species displays a warm metallic sheen that glows beautifully under aquarium lighting—creating a stunning focal point in planted aquariums.

Compact, slow-growing, and hardy, ‘Red Catherine’ thrives in both low- and high-tech aquascapes, making it a top choice for collectors who seek rich color and refined texture in their underwater landscapes.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Red Catherine’

  • Common Name: Buce Red Catherine

  • Brilliant Coloration: Deep red to bronze leaves with a subtle metallic shimmer that intensifies under RGB or high-intensity lighting.

  • Elegant Leaf Shape: Slender, elongated leaves that give an airy, refined look in aquascapes.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Thrives in a wide range of water conditions with minimal maintenance.

  • Epiphytic Nature: Attaches easily to rocks, driftwood, or hardscape—rhizome should not be buried.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium; higher light enhances red and copper tones.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional, but improves coloration and growth rate.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; secure to hardscape with thread or aquarium-safe glue.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required—absorbs nutrients directly from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for paludariums and humid terrarium displays.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds warm red and bronze hues that contrast beautifully with green plants and mosses.

  • Perfect for creating visual balance and color depth in both nano and larger aquascapes.

  • Ideal centerpiece or accent plant for nature-style and collector layouts.

  • Enhances the luxurious, natural ambiance of any aquarium setup.

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, all of our Bucephalandra species are 100% farm-raised—never taken from the wild. Each plant originates ex-vitro (grown from sterile lab culture) before being transitioned to hydroponic growth in our controlled environment. This ensures exceptional quality, pest-free plants, and sustainable preservation of Borneo’s native ecosystems.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose Bare-Root or Potted plants:

  • 1 Bare-Root: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Bare-Root: Approximately 30+ leaves total

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 60+ leaves total

  • 1 Potted: Approximately 10+ leaves

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 100+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.
