products.title: Amazon Frogbit
products.body_html:
🌿 Amazon Frogbit – Floating Beauty for Aquariums & Ponds

Amazon Frogbit  is a popular floating plant prized for its rounded, spongy green leaves and long, flowing roots that dangle gracefully into the water column. This South American native brings a natural, serene aesthetic to both freshwater aquariums and outdoor ponds, while also serving important functional benefits.

With its ability to absorb excess nutrients, shade the water, and provide shelter for fish and fry, Amazon Frogbit is both beautiful and beneficial. It’s a fast grower and easy to maintain, making it an excellent choice for aquarists of all skill levels.

✨ Key Features:

  • Common Name: Amazon Frogbit

  • Growth Form: Floating plant

  • Leaf Shape: Rounded, spongy, vibrant green leaves

  • Root System: Long, hair-like roots that reach deep into the water

Amazon Frogbit grows quickly under optimal conditions. Be sure to trim as needed to prevent surface overgrowth or blockage of light to submerged plants. Not suitable for tanks with strong surface agitation or high water flow.

products.title: Rotala Pearl Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌸 Rotala Pearl Vitro Culture®

Rotala sp. 'Pearl' – A Miniature Gem for Nano Aquascapes (Sold in 4-Inch Bag or 2.5-Inch Cup)

Rotala sp. 'Pearl', also known as Rotala macrandra 'Pearl', is a refined, miniature stem plant celebrated for its tiny, downward-curved leaves and graceful coloration that transitions from green to pink and violet-purple under high light. This delicate selection of Rotala macrandra resembles Rotala macrandra 'Mini Type 2', yet maintains a distinct appearance with its shorter, finely curved leaves—creating a soft and elegant texture ideal for detailed aquascapes.

Perfect for nano aquariums, midground groupings, or foreground accents, Rotala Pearl offers aquascapers a rare balance of elegance and color versatility. Under optimal CO₂ and nutrient-rich conditions, it rewards you with compact, lush growth and brilliant color depth.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Rotala macrandra Koehne

  • Synonym: Rotala sp. 'Pearl'

  • Family: Lythraceae

  • Genus: Rotala

  • Common Name: Rotala Pearl

  • Coloration: Green to pink to purple tones under strong lighting

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting: High lighting recommended for compact growth and vibrant hues

  • CO₂ Requirement: Beneficial and highly recommended

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for healthy root and stem development

  • Temperature Tolerance: 64°F – 86°F (18°C – 30°C)

  • pH Range: 4.0 – 7.0

  • Carbonate Hardness: 0 – 12°dKH

  • General Hardness: 0 – 30°dGH

  • Propagation: By cuttings

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🧫 Vitro Culture® Advantages

  • Lab-grown and cultivated under sterile conditions

  • 100% free from snails, algae, and harmful pesticides

  • Perfect for sensitive aquariums, including shrimp tanks

  • Ensures genetic purity and consistent, healthy growth

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds a soft, fine texture to midground layouts

  • Ideal for nano aquariums and detailed scapes

  • Displays captivating color gradients under CO₂ and strong light

  • Creates elegant transitions between foreground carpeting and taller stems

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • 1 Vitro Culture® portion in a 4-inch sealed bag or 2.5-inch cup

  • Fresh, healthy, and ready to plant directly into your aquarium

⚠️ Disclaimer

Rotala sp. 'Pearl' is best suited for experienced aquascapers using CO₂ injection and high-intensity lighting. Without these conditions, growth may slow, and coloration may remain green. Trim regularly to encourage dense, bushy growth.

Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus', commonly known as Variegated Lucky Bamboo, is an elegant and low-maintenance plant that brings a touch of serenity and natural beauty to any space. Despite its common name, it’s not a true bamboo but a resilient species from the Dracaena family, prized for its striking green leaves edged with creamy white or yellow variegation.

Perfect for both aquariums and decorative indoor arrangements, this plant thrives in water or moist soil, making it ideal for homes, offices, or feng shui displays.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus'

  • Common Name: Variegated Lucky Bamboo

  • Appearance: Elegant green stalks with glossy leaves featuring creamy white margins

  • Versatile Growth: Can be grown in water, hydroponic setups, or soil

  • Symbolism: Represents luck, prosperity, and positive energy in feng shui culture

  • Low Maintenance: Ideal for beginners and busy plant owners

🌱 Growth & Care

  • Lighting: Prefers bright, indirect light but tolerates low-light conditions

  • Watering: Keep roots submerged in clean, chlorine-free water; change weekly

  • Temperature: Thrives in 65–90°F (18–32°C)

  • Humidity: Moderate to high humidity preferred

  • Substrate: Can grow in soil, pebbles, or water with proper support

  • Fertilization: Use a mild liquid fertilizer monthly during growing season

🖼️ Display Ideas

  • Perfect for modern home decor, office desks, or zen garden arrangements

  • Combine multiple stems in glass vases or decorative pots for a lush, elegant look

  • Ideal for feng shui placement to invite harmony and prosperity

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as fresh-cut, unrooted individual plants:
1 Plant: Approximately 10+ inches tall, ready for water or soil placement.

⚠️ Disclaimer

This plant is shipped fresh-cut and unrooted. Upon arrival, place the stem in clean, chlorine-free water to encourage root development. Avoid direct sunlight to prevent leaf burn.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Plant Owner

Certain plant varieties may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release houseplants or aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Aponogeton Ulvaceus
products.body_html:
Aponogeton ulvaceus – Flowing Green Elegance for Large Aquascapes

Aponogeton ulvaceus is a spectacular bulb plant renowned for its vibrant green, ruffled leaves that sway gracefully in the current, creating a captivating underwater display. Native to Madagascar, this fast-growing species is ideal for larger aquariums where its long, arching foliage can shine as a dramatic centerpiece.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Aponogeton ulvaceus

  • Common Name: Ulvaceus Bulb Plant

  • Origin: Madagascar

  • Leaf Length: Up to 60 cm (24 inches) or more

  • Color: Bright to translucent green

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional but enhances growth and color

  • Placement: Midground to background (best in large tanks)

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Water Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.5–7.5

  • Tank Size Recommendation: 20 gallons or larger

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (feeds from bulb and water column)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No

💧 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds dynamic movement with long, ruffled leaves

  • Creates a lush, natural look in open-style or biotope aquascapes

  • Ideal as a focal plant in larger tanks

  • Provides hiding spots and cover for fish

🌼 Propagation & Growth:

Aponogeton ulvaceus reproduces via bulb division and occasionally seeds. It may undergo dormancy after blooming, especially in cooler water—reduce lighting and stop fertilizing during this phase, then resume normal care to trigger regrowth.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

At Aquarium Plants Factory®, we offer two options to suit every aquarist’s preference:

  • Sprouted Bulb With Leaf – for a guaranteed head start.

  • Bulb Only Without Leaf – for experienced hobbyists who prefer to grow from a dormant stage.

⚠️ Important Note:

Bulbs without leaves are healthy and capable of sprouting under stable and proper conditions, usually within a few weeks. However, successful sprouting requires aquarist experience and consistent parameters.
If you’re unsure or want an immediate result, we strongly recommend choosing the Sprouted Bulb With Leaf.

products.title: Ammannia Gracilis
products.body_html:
Ammannia Gracilis, also known as Large Ammannia, Red Ammannia, or Pink Ammannia, is a stunning stem plant that has been popular among aquarists for years. Often mislabeled as Nesaea crassicaulis in trade, true Ammannia crassicaulis is a similar species that turns yellowish-green to reddish under water but is no longer commonly farmed as an aquarium plant.

Here at Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate Ammannia Gracilis emerged. When grown emerged, the leaves are green, but they change to a vibrant red color when adapted to submerged life.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Colors: Ammannia Gracilis is renowned for its brilliant red and pink hues, which are particularly vibrant under high lighting conditions. When grown emersed, its leaves are green, but they transform to a rich red when submerged.
  • Distinctive Leaves: This plant has thin, elongated leaves that grow up to 12 cm long underwater. The leaves are brown-red on top and violet underneath, adding to its unique appearance.

Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires high lighting to achieve its vibrant colors. Medium lighting may result in a more yellow or greenish appearance.
  • CO2 and Nutrients: While CO2 supplementation is not mandatory, it is highly recommended for optimal growth and coloration. Regular fertilization with macro and micronutrients supports its rapid growth.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral water with a pH range of 5.0-7.5. Ideal water temperatures are between 72-82°F (22-28°C). It enjoys temperatures over 24°C but can tolerate up to 32°C.
  • Soft Water: Rather soft water is beneficial, though it can also thrive in medium-hard water with CO2 supplementation.

Planting and Propagation:

  • Substrate: Plant in a nutrient-rich substrate to ensure robust growth. Ammannia Gracilis thrives best when spaced about 2 inches apart to allow for proper light penetration.
  • Propagation: Easily propagated through stem cuttings. Cut healthy stems and replant them in the substrate. This plant is also propagated by stem-tip and side-shoot cuttings.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Pruning: Regular trimming is essential to prevent the plant from overshadowing other species in the aquarium. Trim the plant when it reaches 4-6 inches below the water surface to encourage bushier growth.
  • Water Flow: Avoid high water flow areas as Ammannia Gracilis prefers still or slow-moving water environments.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The vibrant colors and unique growth pattern of Ammannia Gracilis make it an excellent focal point or background plant in aquascapes. It looks best as a group in the middle of larger aquariums and works nicely for "plant streets" in Dutch-style tanks.
  • Easy Care: Despite its requirement for high lighting and CO2, it is relatively easy to care for with the right setup, making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.

Conclusion:

Elevate your aquarium with the stunning Ammannia Gracilis. Its vibrant red and pink colors, combined with its graceful growth pattern, make it a must-have for any aquascape enthusiast. With proper care and maintenance, Ammannia Gracilis will transform your aquarium into a vivid underwater paradise.

products.title: Samolus Valerandi
products.body_html:
Samolus Valerandi, also known by its trade name Samolus Parviflorus, is a versatile and attractive aquatic plant that adds a delicate touch to both aquariums and paludariums. This adaptable species is characterized by its lush rosettes of small, bright green leaves, resembling miniature lettuce, which bring a natural elegance to any setup. With its ability to grow both submerged and emersed, it is a favorite among aquascapers looking for a versatile plant to enhance their aquascapes.

Key Features:

  • Compact Rosette Growth: Forms small, dense clusters of bright green leaves that create a lush, leafy appearance, perfect for adding texture and depth to aquascapes.
  • Adaptable Plant: Thrives in a variety of conditions and can grow submerged in aquariums or emersed in paludariums, terrariums, and ripariums, making it suitable for a range of aquascaping styles.
  • Perfect for Foreground and Midground: The compact nature of Samolus Valerandi makes it ideal for use as a foreground or midground plant, bringing a vibrant green accent to your aquascape.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Grows best under moderate lighting, which encourages healthy, compact growth and leaf development. It can also adapt to both low and high lighting levels.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers temperatures between 68°F to 82°F (20°C to 28°C) and a pH level of 6.0 to 7.5. A nutrient-rich substrate and regular fertilization will promote lush, vigorous growth.

Aquarium and Paludarium Benefits:

  • Natural Look and Feel: With its bright green color and rosette structure, this plant enhances the visual appeal of any aquarium or paludarium, complementing various aquascaping themes.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Helps absorb excess nutrients and stabilize water conditions, contributing to a healthy, balanced aquatic environment.
  • Shelter and Habitat: Provides cover and grazing areas for small fish, shrimp, and other aquatic inhabitants, fostering a natural and stress-free habitat.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Low Maintenance: A hardy and easy-care plant that suits both beginner and experienced aquarists. It requires minimal upkeep while maintaining its compact, attractive shape.
  • Easy Propagation: Propagates through offshoots and runners, allowing for quick and easy spread within your aquatic setup.

Conclusion:

Samolus Valerandi (Samolus Parviflorus) is a versatile, low-maintenance plant that brings a touch of greenery and a natural aesthetic to any aquascape. Whether you want to add a bright accent to your aquarium's foreground or create a lush edge in a paludarium, this adaptable species will thrive and enhance the beauty of your setup. Add the delicate charm and versatility of Samolus Valerandi to your aquatic landscape and enjoy the vibrant look and natural benefits it provides.

products.title: Hydrotriche Hottoniiflora
products.body_html:
Hydrotriche hottoniiflora is an aquatic plant species that belongs to the family Hydrocharitaceae. It is commonly known as the feathered water sprite or the water nymph.

This plant is native to tropical Africa and is widely distributed throughout the continent. It is a popular aquarium plant due to its delicate, feathery leaves that add an elegant touch to any aquascape. Hydrotriche hottoniiflora is also known for its fast growth rate, making it an excellent choice for beginners who want to quickly fill in their aquarium with lush vegetation.

In addition to its aesthetic appeal, Hydrotriche hottoniiflora is also a beneficial plant for the aquarium ecosystem. It helps to oxygenate the water, absorb excess nutrients, and provide hiding places for fish and invertebrates.

To successfully grow Hydrotriche hottoniiflora in an aquarium, it requires moderate to high lighting and nutrient-rich water. It can be propagated by cutting the stem and replanting it, and it is relatively easy to care for.

Overall, Hydrotriche hottoniiflora is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of elegance and benefit the ecosystem of their aquarium.

products.title: Hydrocotyle Leucocephala Variegated
products.body_html:
Hydrocotyle leucocephala 'Variegated', a hybrid version of the common Brazilian Pennywort, is a popular stem plant admired for its rapid growth and vibrant green leaves with unique variegated patterns. This aquatic plant is a favorite among aquarists for its aesthetic appeal and low-maintenance nature.

  • Variegated Leaves: Features vibrantly colorful green leaves with distinctive variegated patterns, adding a unique touch to any aquarium.
  • Rapid Growth: Grows quickly along with runners, making it ideal for filling in spaces and creating lush aquascapes.
  • Lily Pad Resemblance: Leaves closely resemble lily pads in shape and color, enhancing the natural look of the tank.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in medium to high lighting conditions, promoting healthy growth and maintaining the variegated leaf patterns.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C) and slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds visual interest and a splash of color to aquascapes with its unique variegated leaves and fast-growing nature.
  • Low Maintenance: Requires minimal care, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.
  • Versatile Placement: Ideal for midground or background placement, providing depth and texture to the aquarium layout.

Care and Maintenance:

  • CO2 and Nutrients: Benefits from CO2 supplementation and a nutrient-rich substrate to support robust growth and vibrant coloration.
  • Pruning: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages bushier growth.

Unique Hybrid Traits:

  • Variegated Reversion: The variegated pattern can revert to the common green appearance, but the hybrid gene remains, allowing for potential reversion in future generations.

Conclusion:

Hydrocotyle leucocephala 'Variegated' is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of uniqueness and vibrant color to their freshwater aquariums. Its fast growth, easy care requirements, and striking variegated leaves make it a standout addition to any aquascape. Enhance your aquarium with the beauty and dynamic appeal of Hydrocotyle leucocephala 'Variegated' and enjoy a lush, vibrant underwater environment.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Spiralis

Cryptocoryne Spiralis ( Crypt ) is one of the most popular, and widely available aquatic plants in the aquarium hobby. It has several color variations, and it can be found in green, red, pink, bronze and brown colors. The size of the leaves can vary greatly, and even the texture of the leaves can differ from plant to plant. Crypt Wendtii is an extraordinarily hardy plant, and it will thrive under low lighting and in both soft and hard water conditions. It is an excellent plant for both experienced and beginner aquarists.

products.title: Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata'
products.body_html:
🌿 Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata' – Unique Serrated Leaf Form

Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata' is a rare and distinctive form of the popular Bacopa monnieri. While the standard variety is known for its smooth, rounded leaves, the 'Serrata' variant stands out with its delicately serrated, elongated foliage. This subtle difference creates extra texture and depth in aquascapes, making it a favorite among collectors and aquascaping enthusiasts who want something special.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata'

  • Common Name: Serrated Bacopa, Serrated Moneywort, Water Hyssop Variant

  • Common Bacopa vs. 'Serrata':

    • Common Bacopa monnieri → Smooth, rounded leaves with a softer look.

    • Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata' → Slightly elongated leaves with serrated margins, giving a more textured, refined appearance.

  • Growth Habit: Upright stem plant that thrives both submerged and emersed.

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium lighting is sufficient, though higher light enhances leaf definition.

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: Benefits from CO₂ and nutrient-rich conditions but remains hardy without them.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low–medium, higher light improves form.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required, but promotes faster growth and lush leaves.

  • Growth Method: Stem plant; grows vertically, making it perfect for midground or background placement.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (can be anchored or left floating until rooted).

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes — will adapt beautifully in paludariums or humid greenhouse setups.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds a textured, refined look compared to smooth-leaved Bacopa.

  • Works beautifully as a midground accent or background filler.

  • Excellent for contrast planting alongside smooth-leaved Bacopa or fine-leaved stems like Rotala or Cabomba.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Choose from our selection of fresh-cut aquarium plants, available in three convenient formats to suit your needs:

  • Loose Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6 stems of loose, fresh-cut plants without roots.

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6 fresh-cut plants without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight.

  • Potted Plants: About 6 fresh-cut plants without roots, potted in a net pot with rock wool.

⚠️ Disclaimer

As with all stem plants, Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata' may experience minor leaf melt or trimming after shipping. Simply replant healthy stems, and new growth will quickly establish.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.body_html:

Cryptocoryne petchii 'Pink' is a captivating, slow-growing aquarium plant known for its soft blush-pink to reddish-brown hues. Native to Sri Lanka, this variety adds gentle color and texture to the aquascape, making it an ideal choice for foreground to midground use. With its delicate appearance and hardy nature, this crypt is perfect for both beginners and experienced aquascapers looking to add warmth and elegance to their planted tanks.

products.title: Java Moss
products.body_html:
Java Moss is a versatile and undemanding freshwater aquarium plant, perfect for enhancing any aquascape. This popular moss can be left floating or attached to surfaces like rocks or driftwood using fine fishing line or cotton thread. Over time, it forms lush mats that offer excellent hiding places for invertebrates and baby fish, as well as adding a low foreground texture to the aquarium.

Key Features:

  • Versatile Attachment: Can be floated freely or securely attached to rocks, driftwood, and other surfaces.
  • Dense Growth: Forms thick mats that provide natural shelter for aquatic life and add visual depth to the tank.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Temperature: Thrives in cool temperatures, ideally between 20°C to 25°C.
  • Lighting: Prefers low to medium light conditions, making it adaptable to various aquarium setups.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Habitat Creation: Provides essential hiding spots and breeding grounds for invertebrates and baby fish.
  • Easy Maintenance: Requires minimal care, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

Conclusion: Java Moss is an excellent addition to any freshwater aquarium, known for its resilience and versatility. Its ability to thrive in a range of conditions, combined with its aesthetic appeal, makes it a top choice for creating a natural, healthy environment for your aquatic pets. Add Java Moss to your tank for a beautiful, low-maintenance enhancement that benefits both the look and the ecosystem of your aquarium.

  • Sold as a small loose portion, approx. 2" x 2" in size
  • Perfect to attached to driftwood, rock, or stone

products.title: Utricularia Graminifolia Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Utricularia graminifolia Vitro Culture® – Bright Green Foreground Carpet Plant

Sold as: 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag or 2.5-inch cup (depending on availability)

Utricularia graminifolia, commonly known as UG or Grass-leaved Bladderwort, is one of the most popular foreground plants in aquascaping. Famous for its soft, grass-like appearance, UG forms a dense, bright green carpet that brings a natural meadow effect to planted tanks. Despite belonging to the bladderwort family (carnivorous plants), UG is completely safe for aquariums—even for newborn baby shrimp—as its tiny traps only target microscopic organisms.

This plant is especially valued in nano tanks, Iwagumi layouts, and Nature-style aquascapes for its compact size, fluffy texture, and vibrant green coloration.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Utricularia graminifolia

  • Synonyms: Utricularia graminifolia Vahl

  • Common Name: UG, Grass-leaved Bladderwort

  • Family: Lentibulariaceae

  • Leaf Color: Bright green

  • Growth Form: Dense, stolon-forming carpet

  • Unique Trait: Safe for fish and shrimp—even baby shrimp—despite being a carnivorous species

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium–high lighting for compact growth

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Recommended for best results

  • Growth Method: Spreads through fine stolons, forming a continuous carpet

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required – thrives in nutrient-rich, fine-grain soil

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, especially effective with the Dry Start Method (DSM) for rapid establishment

  • Water Parameters: 72–82°F (22–28°C), pH 6.0–7.5

Propagation occurs naturally through runners, allowing UG to fill the foreground with lush greenery once established.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates a lawn-like carpet in the foreground of aquascapes

  • Perfect for nano tanks and Iwagumi layouts

  • Adds a fluffy, bright green contrast against stone and driftwood

  • Harmless and shrimp-safe, making it ideal for community and shrimp tanks

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One Vitro Culture® portion of Utricularia graminifolia

  • Sold as a 4-inch sealed bag or a 2.5-inch cup, depending on availability (customer cannot choose).

  • Lab-grown in sterile gel/liquid, free from snails, algae, pathogens, and pesticides.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Utricularia graminifolia can be a challenging plant for beginners, as it requires stable water parameters, CO₂, and strong lighting for best results. Pictures are for reference only—plants may vary in size, shape, and color depending on growing conditions.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please do not release Utricularia graminifolia or any aquatic plants into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. Check local regulations before purchase.

products.title: Ludwigia Marilia
products.body_html:
🌿 Ludwigia marilia

Rare South American stem plant with round leaves and unique coloration

Ludwigia marilia is a rare and highly sought-after aquatic plant from the surroundings of Marília in São Paulo, Brazil. This plant has not yet been determined to species and is extremely scarce in the aquarium trade, making it a true collector’s item. At Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, we cultivate this plant in its emersed form where the leaves remain green, but once submerged, they transform into light green to light orange tones, especially at the shoot tips.

With its roundish-elliptical leaves and fast growth habit, Ludwigia marilia is an outstanding choice for aquascapers seeking a plant that combines rarity, elegance, and vibrant color contrasts.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia sp. “Marilia”

  • Origin: Marília, São Paulo, Brazil

  • Leaf Shape: Round to elliptical, about 3 cm long and 2 cm wide

  • Color Range: Light green to light orange tones, with reddish hues under strong light

  • Growth Habit: Fast-growing, upright stems reaching 50+ cm, capable of floating when untrimmed

  • Rarity: Rare in trade, not commonly available elsewhere

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Difficulty: Easy

  • Growth Speed: Fast

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to strong

  • CO₂: Not required (but benefits from stable nutrient supply)

  • Water Hardness (Max): Medium

  • Recommended Care Height: 15–60 cm

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for best performance

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation: By head cuttings or partial cuttings, producing numerous side shoots

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds a rare and unique focal point with roundish leaves and soft coloration

  • Works beautifully as a background grouping in larger aquariums (30–50 cm height)

  • In open tanks, develops attractive floating leaves, forming a natural thicket on the water surface

  • Suitable for South American biotope aquariums

📦 What You’ll Receive:
Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6 loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.

  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6 fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.

  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6 fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

⚠️ Disclaimer:
As with many stem plants, some leaf loss or melting may occur during shipping and transition. With proper care and pruning, Ludwigia marilia adapts quickly and produces fresh submerged growth.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner.
Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Anubias Congensis Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Anubias congensis in Vitro Culture® – Sleek, Upright Anubias with Tropical Elegance

Anubias congensis is a visually striking aquatic plant known for its elongated, lance-shaped leaves and upright growth habit. Native to the Congo Basin, this species brings a touch of the tropics to any freshwater aquarium, offering both structure and movement. Its vertical form makes it a perfect choice for midground or background placement, particularly in larger tanks or natural aquascapes.

Now available in a 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag, each pack contains multiple plantlets cultivated using advanced tissue culture methods in vitamin-enriched gel or liquid. This format ensures the plants are 100% free from snails, algae, pesticides, and pathogens, making it safe for shrimp tanks, community aquariums, and sensitive environments.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Anubias congensis

  • Common Name: Congensis Anubias

  • Leaf Shape: Long, narrow, sword-like leaves

  • Growth Habit: Upright, rhizome-based plant

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sterile bag with multiple plantlets

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate

  • Difficulty Level: Easy – low maintenance and adaptable

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Low to medium (resistant to algae under proper conditions)

  • CO₂: Not required

  • Substrate: Not needed – attach rhizome to wood, rock, or decor (never bury)

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5 | Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • Fertilization: Occasional liquid dosing improves color and longevity

🧪 Vitro Culture® Benefits:

  • 100% Free from Snails, Algae, Pathogens, and Pesticides

  • Sterile & Clean: Safe for shrimp and nano aquariums

  • Ready to Plant: Rinse gel, gently separate, and attach to hardscape

  • Extended Shelf Life: Can be stored under soft light until ready to use

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Vertical Leaf Structure: Adds height and flow to planted layouts

  • Ideal for Natural & Biotope Tanks: A great fit for Congo or river-themed scapes

  • Durable & Versatile: Thrives in a variety of freshwater conditions

  • Low-Tech Friendly: Minimal care required for long-term success

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple Anubias congensis plantlets, ready for your next aquascaping project.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Images are for reference only. Leaf size and shape may vary with tank conditions. Always attach the rhizome above substrate to prevent rot.

products.title: Anubias Minima Variegated
products.body_html:
Anubias barteri var. glabra 'Variegated' (Anubias Minima Variegated) is a striking aquatic plant known for its unique lanceolate leaf shape and beautiful white variegation. This undemanding epiphyte is a stunning addition to any aquarium, adding both texture and visual interest with its distinctive foliage and hardy nature. Sometimes referred to as Anubias minima 'Variegated' or 'Variegata', this plant offers a touch of elegance to both beginner and experienced aquascapes.

Key Features:

  • Unique Variegation: Displays a beautiful white pattern on its leaves, adding a delicate touch to the overall greenery. The variegation can vary, sometimes showing pure green leaves.
  • Narrow Lanceolate Leaves: Characterized by horizontally spreading blades that grow to approximately 12 cm in length and 2.8 cm in width, creating a bushy and dense appearance over time.
  • Sturdy Growth Form: Forms a creeping rhizome with long, branched adhesive roots, supporting upright petioles that enhance the plant’s structured, bushy habit.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Epiphyte Growth: Can be easily attached to hardscape elements like driftwood, rocks, or décor, without needing to be planted in substrate.
  • Easy to Maintain: Thrives in low to moderate light and does not demand CO2 supplementation, making it an excellent choice for low-maintenance aquariums.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5 and temperatures between 68°F to 82°F (20°C to 28°C).

Aquarium and Pond Benefits:

  • Natural Aesthetic Appeal: The variegated white coloration and narrow leaf structure provide a stunning contrast to other aquarium plants, making it a standout centerpiece or complement in planted tanks.
  • Hardy and Versatile: Tolerates a range of water conditions, making it suitable for both beginner aquarists and seasoned hobbyists.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Slow and Steady Growth: Requires minimal pruning due to its slow-growing nature. Over time, the rhizome produces side shoots that develop into a lush, dense plant.
  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cutting the rhizome, allowing aquarists to share or spread its beauty throughout the aquarium.

Conclusion:

Anubias barteri var. glabra 'Variegated' is a rare and eye-catching plant with striking variegated leaves and a graceful narrow shape. Its ease of care and versatility make it an excellent addition to aquascapes of all styles. Bring the elegance of this unique plant to your underwater garden, and enjoy the dynamic beauty it adds to your aquatic environment.

products.title: Azolla (Fairy Moss)
products.body_html:
🍃 Azolla (Fairy Moss) – Floating Water Fern for Natural Filtration & Shade

Azolla caroliniana, commonly known as Fairy Moss, Water Fern, Mosquito Fern, Duckweed Fern, or Water Moss, is a fast-growing, floating aquatic fern that adds natural beauty and ecological function to ponds, aquariums, and outdoor water features. Its tiny, overlapping leaves form a delicate mat on the water’s surface, creating a soft, textured blanket of green that turns red or bronze under strong sunlight or cool temperatures.

Known for its ability to absorb excess nutrients, shade the water surface, and inhibit algae growth, Azolla is a favorite among aquarists and pond keepers for its low maintenance and high impact. It also provides excellent cover for shrimp, small fish, and tadpoles, while helping to reduce mosquito breeding.

✨ Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Azolla caroliniana

  • Common Names: Fairy Moss, Water Fern, Mosquito Fern, Duckweed Fern, Water Moss

  • Growth Form: Small, free-floating aquatic fern

  • Leaf Color: Bright green; turns reddish in high light or cool weather

  • Light Demand: Low to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Placement: Surface-floating plant (no substrate required)

🌿 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature: 59–86°F (15–30°C)

  • pH Range: 5.5–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to moderate

  • Lighting: Full sun to moderate light; color intensifies in bright light

🌊 Aquarium & Pond Benefits:

  • Natural Filtration: Absorbs ammonia, nitrates, and other waste

  • Algae Control: Reduces light penetration to help limit algae growth

  • Shade & Shelter: Provides protection and cover for shrimp, fry, and surface-dwelling species

  • Eco-Friendly: Helps reduce mosquito breeding in still waters

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • Sold as a Small Bare-Root Portion:
    Each portion measures approximately 2" x 2" and contains 20+ small floating plants, ideal for immediate use in aquariums or ponds.

    Simply place on the water’s surface and watch it grow and spread under proper conditions.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Azolla grows rapidly and may require regular thinning in well-lit or nutrient-rich environments. Not recommended for tanks with strong surface agitation or high flow. May be restricted in some regions—please check local regulations before purchasing.

featured_image.alt: One of the notable features of Azolla is its unique growth habit. It forms small floating clusters or mats on the water's surface, creating a green carpet-like appearance. The plant consists of tiny, scale-like leaves that are densely packed together, providing a visually appealing texture.

products.title: Rotala Sunset
products.body_html:
Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' is a beautiful aquatic plant that adds a vibrant and colorful element to any freshwater aquarium. This plant features long, thin leaves that can range from green, orange-red to purple tone, depending on the light and nutrient conditions. As a slow-growing plant, Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' requires moderate to high lighting and regular fertilization to thrive. Its vibrant colors make it a popular choice for aquascaping, providing a visually appealing contrast to other plants in the tank. In addition to its aesthetic appeal, this plant also provides valuable benefits to aquariums, including oxygenation and nutrient absorption, making it a valuable asset to any aquatic ecosystem. With its stunning appearance and practical benefits, Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' is a prized possession among aquatic plant enthusiasts and a must-have for any planted tank.

There are different Rotala ramosior species on the market, such as Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' or Rotala ramosior 'Florida'. Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' has rounder leaves and Rotala ramosior 'Florida' has more pointy leaves.

products.title: Cuphea Anagalloidea
products.body_html:
Cuphea anagalloidea is a delicate small stem plant that requires a lot of care.
The brilliant shade of scarlet that may be found on the upper surface of the submerged leaves is brought out to its fullest potential when exposed to bright light. This plant was initially introduced into the aquarium hobby under the name Rotala sp. Araguaia; however, it was later determined that it belongs to the Cuphea species. Cuphea Anagalloidea can only grow vertically. It looks best when used in a group in the middleground as a color accent. The upper edges of the leaves turn a bright crimson to orange-red color under high lighting, but the stem remains green. Sufficient lighting, with plenty CO2 and nutrients, and soft water are all recommended.

products.title: Bolbitis Heudelotii Difformis
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis'

A compact, ruffled twist on the African Water Fern

Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis' is a rare and compact variant of the classic African Water Fern, cherished by aquascapers for its distinctive, finely dissected fronds and slower growth habit. With lush green, feathered foliage and a delicate, curly structure, it brings a graceful, forest-like ambiance to any aquarium setting—especially in shaded or low-flow areas.

This species is epiphytic by nature and thrives when attached to hardscape such as driftwood or porous rock. Its small, wavy-edged leaves and moderate pace make it ideal for nano tanks, low-tech aquascapes, or aquariums with shrimp and delicate fish species.

🌿 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis'

  • Common Name: Mini African Water Fern or Dwarf Bolbitis

  • Growth Form: Rhizomatous epiphyte

  • Leaf Size: Smaller and more dissected than standard Bolbitis heudelotii

  • Placement: Midground to background; best attached to rock or wood

  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Low to medium light preferred; high light may encourage algae on leaves

  • CO₂: Not required, but enhances growth and coloration

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 5.5 – 7.5

    • Temperature: 68 – 79°F (20 – 26°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • Flow: Gentle to moderate; avoid high turbulence

🌱 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a lush, natural look with minimal maintenance

  • Ideal for shady or low-tech tanks

  • Provides excellent shelter for shrimp and fry

  • Pairs beautifully with mosses, Anubias, and Bucephalandra

✂️ Propagation & Growth

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (rhizome should not be buried)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, under high humidity

  • Propagation occurs by dividing the rhizome. Simply cut between leaf clusters and reattach to hardscape for new growth to begin.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Available in three formats:

  • Bare-Root Plants: 4+ healthy fronds with rhizome, loose for flexible placement

  • Bunched Plants: 4+ fronds tied with foam and weight for easy setup

  • Potted Plants: 4+ fronds rooted in rockwool and net pot for secure anchoring

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner


Please check your state regulations before purchasing. Bolbitis species are generally unrestricted, but always verify for local compliance.

📢 Disclaimer

As a slow-growing fern, Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis' may take time to adjust and establish in new environments. Avoid burying the rhizome in substrate, as it may rot. Attach with thread or glue to wood or rock for best results.

products.title: Helanthium Bolivianum (Chain Sword)
products.body_html:
Helanthium bolivianum, formerly known as Echinodorus bolivianum, is a popular aquatic plant commonly known as the Bolivian chain sword or simply chain sword. It is native to South America and belongs to the Alismataceae family.

The plant is characterized by its long, thin, and slightly curled leaves that can grow up to 50 cm in length. The leaves are bright green and grow in a chain-like pattern, hence the name chain sword. The plant grows from a rhizome and can form dense mats in the aquarium, making it a popular choice for aquascaping.

In terms of care, Helanthium bolivianum is relatively easy to maintain. It prefers moderate to high lighting, a nutrient-rich substrate, and a pH range of 6-8. CO2 injection can help to promote optimal growth. The plant is a heavy root feeder, and regular doses of liquid fertilizers can help to ensure healthy growth.

Propagation of Helanthium bolivianum can be achieved by runners that emerge from the parent plant. These runners can be left to grow into new plants or trimmed and replanted elsewhere in the aquarium. The plant can also be propagated by dividing the rhizome.

Overall, Helanthium bolivianum is a popular and attractive plant that is well-suited to planted aquariums. It can be used to create a carpeted effect in the foreground of an aquarium or as a background plant to add depth and dimension to the tank. Its long, flowing leaves can create a beautiful, natural look in any aquarium setting.

  • Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants or potted plants

products.title: Lagenandra Meeboldii Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Lagenandra meeboldii is a freshwater aquatic plant that is native to Southeast Asia, particularly found in India, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh. It belongs to the family Araceae, which includes other popular aquarium plants like Anubias and Cryptocoryne.

Lagenandra meeboldii is a relatively small plant, with leaves that typically grow to around 10-20 cm in length. The leaves are dark green in color and have a slightly glossy texture, with prominent veins that run from the base to the tip of the leaf. The plant is characterized by its unique shape, with the leaves growing in a rosette pattern that gives it a distinctive and attractive appearance.

In aquariums, Lagenandra meeboldii is often used as a midground or background plant, as its size and shape make it an excellent choice for filling in empty spaces and adding depth to the aquascape. It is a relatively hardy plant that can tolerate a wide range of water conditions, including moderate to high lighting and a variety of substrates.

One of the unique features of Lagenandra meeboldii is its ability to change the color of its leaves based on the conditions in which it is grown. Under high light conditions, the leaves can take on a reddish or purplish hue, while under lower light conditions, they may appear more green.

Overall, Lagenandra meeboldii is a beautiful and versatile aquatic plant that can add a unique and attractive element to any aquarium setup. Its hardy nature, ease of care, and ability to adapt to different lighting conditions make it a popular choice among hobbyists and professionals alike.

products.title: Rorippa Aquatica
products.body_html:
🌿 Rorippa aquatica – Unique Rosette Plant

Rorippa aquatica, scientifically classified as Armoracia (Ammoricia) aquatica, is a fascinating and uncommon rosette-type aquarium plant. Known by several names such as Water Cabbage and Lake Cress, this plant is admired for its finely divided, lace-like green leaves that bring a soft, flowing texture to aquascapes.

Often confused with Armoracia aquatica, this species thrives in nutrient-rich substrates under medium to high light, making it a rewarding addition for aquascapers who want to add variety beyond typical stem plants. Its versatility allows it to shine as both a foreground and midground plant, where it adds natural elegance and depth.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Armoracia (Ammoricia) aquatica

  • Common Names: Rorippa aquatica, Water Cabbage, Lake Cress

  • Synonyms: Sometimes mislabeled as Armoracia aquatica

  • Growth Form: Compact rosette with finely divided, feathery leaves

  • Coloration: Lush green foliage that adds contrast and texture

  • Placement: Ideal for foreground to midground positions in aquariums

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting recommended for compact growth and vibrant leaf development

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Benefits from CO₂ injection and iron-rich liquid fertilizers

  • Growth Method: Slow to moderate growth; develops outward from its rosette center

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required – strong root development depends on a nutrient-rich base

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – adaptable to humid greenhouse or paludarium conditions

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Introduces a delicate, feather-like leaf structure uncommon in most aquarium plants

  • Serves as a natural green focal point in foregrounds and transitions seamlessly into midgrounds

  • Excellent for biotope aquariums that replicate shallow lakes, ponds, or marsh habitats

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut, bunched, or potted plants:

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like many rosette-type aquarium plants, Rorippa aquatica may shed older leaves or experience melt after shipping or transplanting. This is normal — new growth will emerge once the plant has acclimated to its environment.

products.title: Selaginella Erythropus (Ruby Red Clubmoss)
products.body_html:
🌿 Selaginella erythropus – Ruby Red Clubmoss

Selaginella erythropus, also known as Ruby Red Clubmoss, is a unique and striking plant prized for its vibrant dual-color foliage. Its lush green fronds are contrasted by brilliant red undersides, making it a favorite among terrarium enthusiasts and houseplant collectors alike. Native to Central and South America, this species thrives in warm, humid environments, where its creeping habit and radiant colors can be fully appreciated.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Selaginella erythropus

  • Common Name: Ruby Red Clubmoss

  • Unique Bicolor Foliage: Bright green fronds with vivid ruby-red undersides.

  • Creeping Growth: Low-growing, spreading habit perfect for terrariums or as ground cover in humid environments.

  • Humidity Lover: Thrives in warm, high-humidity conditions, making it ideal for terrariums and vivariums.

  • Non-Aquatic Plant: Grows exclusively in terrestrial, high-humidity environments (not suitable for aquariums).

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Prefers medium to bright, indirect light; avoid harsh direct sun.

  • Water & Humidity: Keep soil evenly moist at all times; thrives in humidity above 60%.

  • Growth Method: Best grown in terrariums, paludariums, or as a houseplant in controlled humidity.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required, but a rich, well-draining mix enhances growth.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – it is naturally a terrestrial plant and thrives in emersed conditions.

🖼️ Benefits & Uses:

  • Terrarium Highlight: Adds a vibrant pop of green and red, enhancing contrast with other foliage.

  • Humidity Indicator: Its lush fronds will curl or dry if conditions are too dry, serving as a natural reminder to maintain moisture.

  • Natural Carpet: Works beautifully as creeping ground cover in tropical plant displays.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 6 individual stems without root

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6 stems without root bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 6 stems without root potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Selaginella erythropus is highly dependent on consistent moisture and humidity. If allowed to dry out, it will quickly decline. Ensure proper terrarium or indoor care to maintain its vibrant appearance.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

While this plant is not restricted in the U.S., always grow and dispose of plants responsibly to protect native ecosystems.

products.title: Saururus Cernuus (Lizard's Tail)
products.body_html:
Saururus cernuus – Unique Heartleaf Emerged Plant for Aquariums & Paludariums

Saururus cernuus, commonly known as Lizard’s Tail, is a versatile and visually striking aquatic plant native to North America. Known for its distinctive heart-shaped leaves and elegant upright growth, this plant thrives in high-humidity environments and shallow water conditions, making it an excellent choice for paludariums, ripariums, Wabi-Kusa setups, and transitional zones in aquariums.

This plant is typically offered in its emersed form, allowing it to adapt quickly to a variety of semi-aquatic and marginal aquatic environments. Its foliage can develop a rich, bright green color with a graceful arching habit, and in emersed conditions, it may even produce delicate white flower spikes resembling a lizard’s tail.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Saururus cernuus

  • Common Names: Lizard’s Tail, Heartleaf

  • Leaf Shape: Heart-shaped, bright green, lush foliage

  • Growth Form: Upright stem plant with arching stems and broad leaves

  • Light Requirement: Low to medium

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Temperature Range: 60–82°F (16–28°C)

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; soft to moderate hardness

  • Growth Rate: Medium to fast

  • Placement: Ideal for emersed sections in paludariums, ripariums, and marginal aquarium zones

🧪 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under low to moderate light; high humidity enhances leaf health

  • Temperature: Best between 68–78°F for vigorous growth

  • Substrate: Can grow in wet soil or rooted in rockwool; prefers nutrient-rich base

  • Water Depth: Shallow or marginal; thrives in transitional wet zones

  • Fertilization: General liquid or substrate fertilization helps support lush foliage

💡 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Emersed Appeal: Perfect for paludariums, rimless tanks, and shallow water displays

  • Architectural Form: Adds height, structure, and soft movement to aquascapes

  • Natural Accent: Pairs beautifully with mosses, ferns, and hardscape

  • Seasonal Bloomer: May produce fragrant, white tail-like flowers in emersed setups

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Growth Speed: Moderate to fast

  • Propagation Method: Via stem cuttings or underground rhizome division

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – optimal in emersed form

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Available in three convenient planting formats to suit your aquatic or semi-aquatic design:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approx. 3–4 loose, fresh-cut stems without roots

  • Bunched Plants: Approx. 3–4 fresh-cut stems bundled with plant weight for easy placement

  • Potted Plants: Approx. 3–4 fresh-cut stems potted in rockwool within a net pot

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please do not release Saururus or any aquatic plant into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. While not restricted in the U.S., always follow local guidelines.

🔒 Disclaimer:

Photos shown are sample representations only. Plants may arrive in emersed form and adapt once planted. We do not guarantee plants are free from pests or pesticides unless otherwise specified.

products.title: Alternanthera Ficoidea Rainbow
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' is a vibrant terrarium, vivarium, or bog plant that is sometimes used as an aquatic plant decoration, despite not being a true aquatic species. This plant can be submerged for several months under the right conditions, but it requires periodic emersed growth to restore its energy and continue thriving.

Key Features:

  • Colorful Appearance: Features a stunning array of colors, including shades of red, yellow, and green, adding a rainbow-like effect to any setup.
  • Versatile Habitat: Can be used in terrariums, vivariums, bog gardens, and temporarily in aquariums.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Submerged Growth: Can survive submerged in aquariums for several months. After this period, it should be transitioned to emersed growth.
  • Emersed Growth: Requires high humidity and consistently wet soil when grown above water, especially during recovery phases.
  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which helps maintain its vibrant color palette.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Temporary Aquatic Decoration: Provides an attractive and colorful addition to aquariums for several months, developing roots and sometimes growing a few inches taller during its submerged period.
  • Alternative to Aquatic Plants: Its ability to survive underwater for extended periods makes it a popular alternative for hobbyists seeking unique and colorful decorations.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Periodic Transition: To ensure longevity, alternate the plant between submerged and emersed conditions every few months.
  • High Humidity: When grown emersed, maintain consistently high humidity and keep the soil wet to support recovery and growth.

Conclusion:

Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' is a versatile and visually stunning plant that can enhance the beauty of terrariums, vivariums, bog gardens, and aquariums. Its ability to survive underwater for extended periods makes it a unique choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color to their setups. By alternating between submerged and emersed growth, you can enjoy the vibrant presence of Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' in various environments.

Enhance your terrarium, vivarium, or aquarium with the stunning and versatile Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow', and enjoy its vibrant colors and adaptability.

products.title: Anubias Peacock
products.body_html:
🌿 Anubias nana 'Peacock' – Rare Pointed-Leaf Anubias

Anubias nana 'Peacock' is a rare and highly sought-after variety of Anubias barteri distinguished by its elegant, pointed leaves and thick, waxy texture. Its compact growth habit and hardiness make it an ideal choice for aquascapers who want a durable yet unique focal plant that thrives in a wide range of aquarium conditions.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Anubias barteri var. nana 'Peacock'

  • Leaf Shape: Distinctively pointed, spear-like leaves with a glossy, waxy feel

  • Growth Form: Compact rhizome plant, slow-growing and hardy

  • Coloration: Deep, rich green foliage that retains vibrancy under various lighting conditions

  • Ease of Care: One of the most resilient aquarium plants, perfect for beginners and collectors alike

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light; resists algae when shaded

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required, though growth improves with CO₂ supplementation

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0 – 7.5; temperature 68°F – 82°F (20°C – 28°C)

  • Growth Method: Attach to hardscape such as driftwood or rock; avoid burying rhizome

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, thrives in paludariums and humid terrariums

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Unique Leaf Shape: Pointed, glossy foliage creates striking contrast in aquascapes

  • Versatile Placement: Works well in the foreground or midground when attached to hardscape

  • Low Maintenance: Extremely hardy and slow-growing, making it easy to manage

  • Collector’s Choice: Rare cultivar not commonly available in the trade

💎 Why Choose Anubias 'Peacock'?

Unlike other Anubias varieties with rounded or wavy leaves, Anubias 'Peacock' stands out with its bold, spear-like foliage. Its durability, paired with unique aesthetics, makes it a must-have for aquascaping enthusiasts and plant collectors seeking something special.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Available in multiple forms:

  • Bare-root plants: A small loose portion with approx. 6+ leaves

  • Potted plants: Net pot with rockwool containing approx. 6+ leaves

⚠️ Disclaimer

Do not bury the rhizome in substrate, as this may cause rotting. Instead, attach the plant to rocks, driftwood, or decorations until roots anchor naturally.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Before purchasing, check local regulations on aquatic plants. Dispose of trimmings responsibly—never release aquarium plants into local waterways.

products.title: Pogostemon Sampsonii
products.body_html:
Pogostemon sampsonii is a captivating and rare aquarium plant from southern China, prized for its fine, willow-like foliage and flowing growth habit. Once misidentified as Limnophila punctata 'Blume' and Pogostemon cf. pumilus, this species was accurately classified by aquatic botanist Christel Kasselmann. A member of the Lamiaceae family, Pogostemon sampsonii thrives in both planted aquariums and nature aquascapes, where its soft, delicate texture creates contrast and movement underwater.

Key Features & Benefits

  • 🌿 Elegant Fine-Leaved Structure – Adds soft, flowing texture to the foreground or midground.

  • 💧 Ideal for Aquascaping – Perfect for nature aquariums, Iwagumi layouts, and Dutch-style tanks.

  • 💡 Thrives with CO₂ and Strong Light – Vibrant, lush growth with proper lighting and fertilization.

  • 🐠 Fish and Shrimp Friendly – Provides gentle cover for small aquatic animals.

  • 🌱 Rare Species – A collector's gem recently reclassified and gaining popularity in the hobby.

Transform Your Aquascape with Graceful Movement

Pogostemon sampsonii is ideal for aquarists seeking a plant that combines rarity with visual impact. Its delicate, elongated leaves sway gently in water flow, adding lifelike motion to your fish tank. When cultivated with CO₂ supplementation, nutrient-rich substrate, and high-intensity lighting, this plant forms dense, bushy clusters that enhance the natural feel of your freshwater planted aquarium.

Whether you're a beginner experimenting with aquatic plants or an expert designing a high-end aquascape, Pogostemon sampsonii offers something exceptional—rare lineage, soft texture, and vibrant green coloration that stands out against hardscape or red-leaved background species.

products.title: Echinodorus Uruguayensis Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Echinodorus uruguayensis in Vitro Culture® – Highly Variable Sword Plant with Elegant, Flowing Foliage

Echinodorus uruguayensis is a highly variable and time-honored species within the aquarium hobby, known under numerous synonyms and trade names including E. africanus, E. horemanii, E. veronikae, and E. viridis. Native to the cooler, fast-flowing rivers of southeastern South America, this species is cherished for its long, flexible, ribbon-like leaves and its many color forms—ranging from medium green to deep red and even blackish hues.

Now available in our sterile Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag, E. uruguayensis is tissue-cultured for a 100% algae-free, snail-free, and pesticide-free start. This makes it ideal for sensitive aquariums, planted displays, and collectors seeking rare genetic lines of this classic sword plant.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Echinodorus uruguayensis

  • Common Trade Names: E. africanus, E. horemanii, E. veronikae, E. viridis

  • Leaf Color: Variable—bottle green, reddish, dark green, to nearly black

  • Growth Form: Rosette with oblong to band-shaped, slightly transparent submersed leaves

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sealed bag with multiple healthy plantlets in sterile gel or liquid

  • Placement: Background (ideal for large aquariums or pond setups)

  • Growth Rate: Moderate to fast under proper conditions

  • Difficulty Level: Medium

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; necessary for robust growth and coloration

  • CO₂: Recommended for best results, especially with red or black forms

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate required; iron supplementation enhances color

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5 | Temperature: 64–75°F (18–24°C)

  • Water Hardness: Tolerant to a wide range, though soft to slightly acidic water is ideal

🌱 Botanical Notes:

  • The plant forms long, unbranched flower stalks and a creeping or underground rhizome system

  • It reproduces via adventitious plantlets on flower stalks and lateral rhizome shoots

  • Submersed growth produces long, flowing leaves; emersed rosettes stay smaller and compact

  • Can overwinter outdoors in temperate climates and thrives in ponds during warm seasons

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% Clean and Pest-Free: No algae, snails, or disease

  • Safe for All Tanks: Especially suitable for shrimp and sensitive species

  • Easy to Store: Keep under light until ready to plant

  • Planting Made Simple: Rinse, divide gently, and plant into nutrient-rich substrate

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Striking Background Centerpiece: Ideal as a focal or solitary plant in large aquariums (300L+)

  • Natural Movement: Long, ribbon-like leaves sway gracefully with current

  • Color Versatility: Complements both green and red plants with its dynamic hues

  • Perfect for Hybrid Projects: A foundational species used in breeding ornamental Echinodorus cultivars

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing healthy Echinodorus uruguayensis plantlets—ready to rinse, acclimate, and plant.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Photos are for reference only. Due to its variability, actual leaf color and size may differ depending on aquarium conditions and plant form. Ensure proper lighting, nutrients, and CO₂ for optimal growth and coloration. Best suited for aquariums of 75 gallons or more.

products.title: Bucephalandra Red Mini
products.body_html:
🌸 Bucephalandra ‘Red Mini’ – Scarlet Gem of Borneo

Bucephalandra ‘Red Mini’ is a striking and compact Bucephalandra variety renowned for its vivid red-bronze foliage and petite size. Originating from the riverbanks of Borneo, this beautiful plant showcases slender, oval leaves with a deep ruby to copper tone that glows under high or RGB lighting. Its miniature growth habit makes it an ideal choice for nano aquascapes, foreground accents, or driftwood details. Slow-growing, hardy, and easy to maintain, ‘Red Mini’ adds a bold yet refined splash of color to any aquascape.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Red Mini’

  • Common Name: Buce Red Mini

  • Brilliant Coloration: Deep red to bronze leaves that shimmer with a metallic sheen under aquarium lighting.

  • Miniature Growth Form: Naturally compact and low-profile—perfect for nano tanks and small aquascapes.

  • Leaf Texture: Smooth, rounded leaves that intensify in color under strong or RGB lighting.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Thrives in a wide range of water parameters and lighting levels.

  • Epiphytic Nature: Attaches easily to driftwood, stones, or hardscape—rhizome must stay above substrate.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light; stronger lighting enhances red hues and metallic reflections.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional, but beneficial for faster growth and richer coloration.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; do not bury the rhizome.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required—absorbs nutrients from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for paludariums and humid terrarium setups.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Infuses vivid red and bronze tones that contrast beautifully with green plants and mosses.

  • Ideal for nano aquascapes, shrimp tanks, or as a bright midground highlight.

  • Perfect for collectors seeking color depth in small, detailed layouts.

  • Adds visual warmth and balance to both low-tech and high-tech aquascapes.

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, all of our Bucephalandra species are 100% farm-raised—never taken from the wild. Each plant originates ex-vitro (grown from sterile lab culture) before being transitioned to hydroponic growth in our controlled environment. This ensures exceptional quality, pest-free plants, and sustainable preservation of Borneo’s native ecosystems.

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

Bucephalandra Godzilla (Kedagang Red) is a bold and visually striking Bucephalandra variety known for its dramatic red undertones, elongated leaves, and rugged, textured surface. products.title: Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark is a captivating and rare aquatic plant that brings a touch of sophistication to any aquarium. Known for its deep, rich hues and unique foliage, this plant is a favorite among aquascapers and aquarium enthusiasts. With its easy care requirements and stunning appearance, Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark is perfect for both novice and experienced aquarists.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark

  1. Deep, Dark Foliage: Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark boasts leaves with a deep, dark green to almost black coloration, creating a striking contrast in your aquarium. The leaves' subtle iridescence adds an extra layer of beauty, making this plant a standout feature in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, and its slow growth rate ensures it maintains its shape without frequent trimming.

  3. Easy to Care For: Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark is a hardy plant that thrives in a range of water conditions. It requires low to moderate lighting and can tolerate various water parameters, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech setups.

  4. Unique Flowers: In addition to its stunning foliage, Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark occasionally produces small, delicate flowers that add an extra layer of beauty to the aquarium. These flowers typically bloom above the water surface.

  5. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, this plant grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for replicating natural biotope aquariums.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark. Too much light can lead to algae growth on its leaves, while too little light may reduce its vibrant coloration.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: While Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark can be planted in substrate, it often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will promote healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance growth but is not strictly necessary.
  • Propagation: This plant can be easily propagated by cutting and replanting its rhizomes. Ensure each cutting has a few leaves and roots for successful propagation.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The deep, dark foliage of Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark creates a stunning visual effect, enhancing the overall beauty of your aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark is a must-have for any aquarist looking to elevate the beauty and biodiversity of their aquarium. Its unique appearance, combined with its ease of care, makes it a standout choice for creating a vibrant and healthy underwater environment.

For more detailed information and to purchase Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark, visit reputable aquatic plant retailers or specialized aquarium forums.

products.title: Nymphaea Yellow Bangpra (Pin waree) | Yellow Hardy Water Lily
products.body_html:
🌼 Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’ (Pin Waree) – Hardy Yellow Water Lily

Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’, also known as ‘Pin Waree’, is a stunning hardy yellow water lily cherished for its sunny golden blooms and graceful floating leaves. Originating from Thailand, this beautiful hybrid combines elegance and durability, producing vibrant flowers that bring warmth and tropical brightness to any pond or water garden. With its consistent blooming habit and easy adaptability, Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’ adds a radiant, cheerful focal point to your aquatic landscape all season long.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’ (Synonym: Nymphaea ‘Pin Waree’)

  • Common Name: Yellow Bangpra Water Lily

  • Type: Hardy Water Lily

  • Flower Color: Bright yellow with soft golden tones and a sunny yellow center

  • Flower Form: Star-shaped blooms with multiple layers of narrow, pointed petals

  • Bloom Size: Medium (4–6 inches / 10–15 cm)

  • Foliage: Rounded green leaves with occasional purplish mottling

  • Blooming Season: May through September (long and free-flowering)

  • Fragrance: Light, pleasant scent

  • Growth Spread: Moderate (up to 3 feet / 90 cm)

🌤️ Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Full sun exposure (6–8 hours daily) for maximum color and bloom production

  • Planting Depth: 12–20 inches (30–50 cm) of water above the rhizome

  • Soil Type: Heavy loam or field soil enriched with 3–5 aquatic fertilizer tablets near the rhizome

  • pH Range: 6.0–8.0 (tolerates both slightly acidic and alkaline water)

  • Temperature Tolerance: Hardy in most climates; can overwinter outdoors when pond does not freeze solid

🖼️ Aquascaping & Pond Benefits:

  • Bright Focal Point: Brilliant yellow blooms add a warm, uplifting tone to any pond design.

  • Healthy Pond Ecosystem: Broad leaves help reduce algae and provide natural shade for fish.

  • Long-Lasting Blooms: Continuous flowering throughout the summer for a vibrant pond display.

  • Low Maintenance: Easy to plant, grow, and propagate, ideal for both beginners and experts.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One young, viable Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’ hardy water lily rhizome with a healthy crown and 1–2 actively growing leaves, ensuring strong establishment and reliable flowering after planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

All water lilies are shipped bare-root (as tubers) and should be planted immediately upon arrival for best results. Bloom and leaf color, size, and shape may vary slightly depending on lighting, water quality, and plant maturity.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner:

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium or pond plants into the wild.

PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

 

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

products.title: Myriophyllum Guyana Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Myriophyllum Guyana is a beautiful and versatile aquatic plant that adds elegance to any aquarium. Known for its delicate, feathery foliage, this plant is a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts looking to create lush, green underwater landscapes.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Myriophyllum Guyana
  • Common Name: Myriophyllum Guyana
  • Family: Haloragaceae
  • Leaf Color: Bright green
  • Growth Form: Feathery, bushy

Unique Characteristics

Myriophyllum Guyana is renowned for its finely divided, bright green leaves that create a soft, feathery appearance. This plant’s graceful and bushy growth makes it an excellent choice for adding texture and depth to your aquarium, creating a natural and serene environment.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure optimal growth for Myriophyllum Guyana, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Moderate to high intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial but not essential
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

Myriophyllum Guyana is relatively easy to care for, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure moderate to high light levels to promote healthy growth.
  • CO2 Injection: While not required, CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and overall health.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports vibrant growth and health.

Aquascaping Uses

Myriophyllum Guyana is highly versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Midground Plant: Ideal for adding density and vibrant green color to the midground area.
  • Background Plant: Can be used to create a lush green backdrop.
  • Accent Plant: Its feathery leaves make it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The bright green, feathery foliage of Myriophyllum Guyana provides excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Myriophyllum Guyana is simple and effective. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Simply cut a healthy stem and replant it in the substrate, where it will develop roots and grow into a new plant.

Myriophyllum Guyana is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a graceful and eye-catching plant. Its delicate, feathery leaves and bushy growth make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

products.title: Bolbitis sp. Buea
products.body_html:
🌿 Bolbitis sp. 'Buea' – Rare African Water Fern with Elegant, Textured Fronds

Bolbitis sp. 'Buea', commonly known as the Buea Fern, is a rare and highly sought-after aquatic fern from the river systems near Buea, Cameroon, in Central Africa. Known for its gracefully arching, deep green fronds with a lightly textured surface, this plant brings a refined and natural look to any aquascape. Like other Bolbitis species, it thrives best when attached to hardscape rather than planted in substrate, making it an excellent choice for driftwood- and stone-centered layouts.

Unlike the more common Bolbitis heudelotii, the Buea Fern has slightly narrower fronds with a delicate, almost lace-like quality, adding a unique dimension to planted tanks.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Bolbitis sp. 'Buea'

  • Synonyms: Didymoglossum erosum

  • Common Name: Buea Fern

  • Origin: Cameroon, Central Africa

  • Growth Form: Rhizome-based aquatic fern with arching, textured fronds

  • Placement: Best suited for midground or background, attached to wood or rock

  • Lighting: Low to moderate light, avoiding direct high-intensity light to prevent algae growth

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: Appreciates CO₂ and nutrient-rich water for optimal growth

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium (avoid strong, direct light)

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ recommended; benefits from regular dosing of liquid fertilizers

  • Growth Method: Rhizome attachment to hardscape (never bury rhizome in substrate)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, in high-humidity terrariums or paludariums

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds lush, natural greenery to shaded aquarium areas

  • Provides shelter for shrimp and small fish

  • Slow-growing and algae-resistant under proper conditions

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as a small portion

  • Small portion: One loose portion, approx. 2" x 2" in size

  • This moss is dark green in color

⚠️ Disclaimer:
Bolbitis species are sensitive to unstable water parameters and can be slow to adapt to new environments. Growth may temporarily stall during acclimation. Avoid burying the rhizome in substrate, as this can cause rot.
