products.title: Nymphaea Director George T. Moore | Violet-Purple Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
Nymphaea 'Director George T. Moore' is a stunning tropical water lily known for its captivating violet-purple flowers and robust growth. This aquatic plant is perfect for adding a splash of vibrant color to any pond or water garden.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Violet-Purple Flowers: Boasts large, star-shaped flowers that are a striking violet-purple, adding a dramatic and elegant touch to your water garden. Each bloom measures 4-6 inches in diameter and is highly fragrant.
  • Lush Foliage: Features broad, rounded green leaves that provide a beautiful contrast to the vivid flowers, enhancing the overall aesthetic of your pond.
  • Prolific Blooming: Blooms profusely from June to frost, ensuring a long-lasting display of color throughout the growing season.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Thrives in full sun, requiring at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily for optimal blooming.
  • Water Depth: Best planted at a depth of 12 to 24 inches in still or slow-moving water.
  • Soil Type: Prefers heavy clay or loam soils. Planting in containers submerged in the pond helps manage its growth and makes seasonal care easier.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Enhances the visual appeal of any pond with its vibrant, showy flowers and lush foliage.
  • Wildlife Attraction: Attracts beneficial wildlife such as bees and dragonflies, contributing to a healthy pond ecosystem.
  • Fragrant Blooms: The flowers emit a pleasant fragrance, adding an extra sensory delight to your garden.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization during the growing season with aquatic plant fertilizer promotes healthy growth and abundant blooming.
  • Pruning: Remove yellowing leaves and spent flowers to encourage new growth and maintain plant health.
  • Winter Care: In cooler climates, treat this tropical lily as an annual or store the tubers in a cool, frost-free area until spring. This involves removing the tubers after the first frost, hosing them off, allowing them to dry for several days, and storing them in jars of distilled water or moist sand at temperatures around 50-55°F.

Conclusion:

Nymphaea 'Director George T. Moore' is a magnificent addition to any water garden, offering vibrant color, fragrant blooms, and a long blooming season. Its ease of care and stunning appearance make it a favorite among both novice and experienced gardeners. Add the captivating beauty of Nymphaea 'Director George T. Moore' to your pond today and enjoy the dynamic transformation it brings to your aquatic environment.

products.title: Nymphaea Tina | Violet Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
🌸 Nymphaea ‘Tina’ – Tropical Day-Blooming Waterlily

Nymphaea ‘Tina’ is one of the most beloved tropical waterlily cultivars, renowned for its deep violet-purple blooms with a distinctive cup-like shape and a sweet, pleasant fragrance. The flowers rise elegantly above the water’s surface, contrasted by light green leaves that display a bronze tint when young.

This free-flowering and viviparous variety not only produces abundant blooms but also creates new plantlets on its leaves, making it an exceptional choice for both beginners and seasoned collectors. Its adaptability to various water depths and pond sizes makes ‘Tina’ a versatile and rewarding addition to any water garden.

  • Striking Blooms: Cup-shaped flowers in rich violet-purple with golden-yellow centers.

products.title: Nymphaea Colorata | Violet-Blue Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
Nymphaea Colorata is a stunning tropical water lily that brings a vibrant splash of color to any pond or water garden. Renowned for its exquisite violet-blue blooms and resilient nature, this water lily is a favorite among aquatic plant enthusiasts.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Violet-Blue Flowers: Nymphaea Colorata boasts large, star-shaped flowers with a captivating violet-blue hue. Each bloom measures approximately 4-6 inches (10-15 cm) across, creating a striking display on the water's surface.
  • Lush Green Foliage: The plant features rounded, bright green leaves that provide a beautiful contrast to its vibrant flowers.
  • Long Blooming Season: This water lily blooms prolifically from late spring through fall, ensuring continuous color in your pond.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Thrives in full sun, requiring at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily for optimal blooming.
  • Water Depth: Best planted at a depth of 12 to 36 inches (30-90 cm) in your pond or water garden.
  • Soil Type: Prefers heavy clay or loam soils. Plant it in a container for best results and place it at the bottom of the pond.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Enhances the visual appeal of any pond with its vibrant violet-blue flowers and lush green foliage.
  • Wildlife Attraction: Attracts beneficial wildlife such as bees and dragonflies, contributing to a healthy pond ecosystem.
  • Fragrant Blooms: The flowers emit a pleasant fragrance, adding a sensory delight to your garden.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization during the growing season with aquatic plant fertilizer promotes healthy growth and abundant blooming.
  • Pruning: Remove yellowing leaves and spent flowers to encourage new growth and maintain plant health.
  • Winter Care: As a tropical water lily, it requires special care in colder climates. In regions with cold winters, it is best to lift the plant and store it in a warm, frost-free area until spring.

Conclusion:

Nymphaea Colorata is a stunning tropical water lily that adds vibrant color and elegance to any pond or water garden. Its prolific blooming, striking violet-blue flowers, and easy care make it an excellent choice for enhancing your aquatic landscape. Add the enchanting Nymphaea Colorata to your pond today and enjoy the continuous beauty it brings to your aquatic environment.

products.title: Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice]
products.body_html:
Tropical water lilies are a beautiful addition to any pond or water garden, and they come in a wide variety of colors and sizes. When purchasing Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice], you will receive a selection of water lilies chosen by the grower based on availability and the best performers for your growing zone.

The packages typically include a variety of colors, such as pink, red, white, and yellow, and may also include various sizes ranging from small to large. The grower will choose the best selection for you based on the zone that you live in, as well as the specific conditions of your pond or water garden.

Tropical water lilies are called "tropical" because they are native to warmer climates and cannot tolerate cold temperatures. They require full sun and warm water to thrive, making them ideal for warmer regions. Tropical water lilies are also known for their large, showy blooms that can reach up to 12 inches in diameter.

When you receive your Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice], you should follow the instructions that come with them for planting and care. It is essential to make sure that you choose the right location for your water lilies, as they require warm water and at least 6 hours of direct sunlight each day to thrive.

Overall, purchasing a Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice] is an excellent way to add beauty and color to your water garden or pond, and you can trust that the grower has chosen the best selection of water lilies for your growing zone and conditions. However, if you live in a colder region, it is important to note that tropical water lilies may not survive the winter outdoors, and you may need to bring them indoors or replace them with hardy water lilies.

products.title: Samolus Parviflorus Red
products.body_html:
Samolus parviflorus 'Red' — A Rare Splash of Red for Your Aquascape

Samolus parviflorus 'Red' is a unique and visually striking aquatic plant prized for its vivid coloration and easy adaptability. Native to the limestone-rich rivers of South China, this rare beauty offers a rosette-style growth pattern and deep red hues when grown submerged—making it an exceptional choice for aquascapers who want to introduce bold, natural tones into their underwater landscapes.

Unlike many "red" plants that require high maintenance, Samolus 'Red' thrives with moderate care, making it accessible for both beginners and experienced hobbyists.

Key Features, Benefits & Growing Tips:

🔴 Stunning Red Coloration:
Emerges green when grown emersed but transitions into brilliant shades of red when fully submerged under moderate to high light.

🌿 Rosette Growth Form:
Creates low, compact clusters perfect for midground accents or foreground highlights in planted aquariums.

💧 Adaptable & Easy to Care For:
Tolerates a wide range of water parameters and grows well with or without CO₂ injection (though CO₂ and strong lighting intensify the red coloration).

✂️ Simple Propagation:
Can be propagated easily by replanting side shoots or separated stems, allowing for fast carpet expansion.

🐠 Perfect For:
Nature aquariums, contrast-driven layouts, biotope-style tanks, and hobbyists wanting a rare, standout species in their collection.

Why Choose Samolus parviflorus 'Red' for Your Planted Aquarium?

Adding depth and vibrant contrast to a planted tank can be challenging—but Samolus parviflorus 'Red' makes it effortless. Its brilliant submerged coloration immediately draws the eye, providing a bold counterbalance to green foliage plants. Hardy, undemanding, and visually captivating, it’s an excellent choice for aquascapers aiming to create a dynamic and colorful aquascape without the intense care often associated with red plants.

Whether you’re crafting a lush nature aquarium, designing a contest-level tank, or simply want something rare and beautiful, Samolus 'Red' is a rewarding and impactful addition.

Product Specifications:

  • Scientific Name: Samolus parviflorus 'Red'

  • Common Name: Samolus Red, Lysimachia parvifolia 'Red'

  • Origin: South China (Guangxi Province)

  • Growth Form: Rosette

  • Height: 5–15 cm

  • Light Requirement: Medium to High

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional (Recommended for best color)

  • Growth Rate: Medium

  • Difficulty: Easy to Moderate

    Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Java Fern Sunrise
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Sunrise’ – Bronze-Tinted Java Fern with Unique Leaf Form

Microsorum pteropus ‘Sunrise’, also known as Java Fern Sunrise, is a rare and eye-catching variety of the classic Java Fern. This cultivar features slender, elongated leaves with subtle three-pointed tips, displaying a beautiful range of coloration from rich green to bronze under the right lighting. Occasionally, new growth may take on a reddish tint, giving your aquascape a soft touch of warmth and contrast.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we grow this plant emersed in our nursery, where it develops healthy structure and vibrant form. Once submerged in your aquarium, it transitions beautifully and thrives with minimal care. Like all Java Ferns, Sunrise is a rhizome-based plant and should be attached to driftwood, stone, or hardscape—never buried in substrate.

Its slow growth and graceful shape make it ideal for aquascapers looking to add texture and gentle movement to midground or background areas in both low- and high-tech setups.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Subtle Red-Bronze Highlights: New leaves may develop reddish or bronze tones under strong light

  • Elegant Growth Form: Slender, narrow leaves with occasional three-pointed tips

  • Attachable Rhizome Plant: Grows best on rocks, driftwood, or decor—no planting required

  • Low Maintenance: Thrives without CO₂ and under low to high lighting

  • Beginner Friendly: Easy to care for and slow-growing, great for low-tech tanks

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus ‘Sunrise’

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus ‘Sunrise’

  • Common Names: Java Fern Sunrise

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or hardscape

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to High

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Performs well under low to high light—reddish tones emerge with brighter light

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: Not required; occasional liquid fertilizer boosts health and color

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation Method: Rhizome division

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 3+ leaf portion, loose rhizome

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 3+ leaf portion bundled with foam and plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 3+ leaf portion, potted in rockwool and net pot

Photos are sample representations. Actual plants may vary in size, shape, or coloration. Unless otherwise specified, we do not guarantee plants are free of pests, pesticides, or exclusively submerged-grown.

products.title: Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy
products.body_html:
🌿 Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy – Compact, Wavy-Leaf Aquatic Plant

Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy is a slow-growing, compact aquarium plant distinguished by its beautifully curled, ruffled leaf edges. This hardy selection of the Cardinal Flower has a very bushy underwater growth form, making it ideal for smaller aquariums and precise aquascaping layouts. While its leaf size is similar to the original Lobelia cardinalis, its denser, more compact habit offers a unique texture and structure for your tank.

At Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, we cultivate Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy in its emersed form, where the tops of the leaves display a fresh green hue while the undersides show a striking red-purple color. When grown submerged, the foliage transitions to lush green, with its distinctive wavy edges adding visual interest. This variety is still relatively new and rarely available in the aquarium trade, making it a sought-after choice for collectors and aquascaping enthusiasts.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Unique Wavy Leaves: Curled, ruffled edges create a textured and decorative look.

  • Compact & Bushy Growth: Perfect for small tanks and foreground to midground placement.

  • Color Variation: Green tops with red-purple undersides in emersed growth; transitions to green when submerged.

  • Rare in Trade: A newer Lobelia cardinalis selection not commonly available.

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting for healthy, compact growth.

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: Benefits from CO₂ injection and balanced fertilization, though not strictly required.

  • Growth Method: Cultivated emersed at Aquarium Plants Factory nursery.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended for strong root development.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes.

  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cuttings, like other stem plants. Trimming the main shoot encourages multiple side shoots.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Works beautifully in dense groupings for Dutch-style layouts.

  • Single, well-ramified plants also make attractive focal points.

Proserpinaca palustris

Also known as Mermaid Weed — An Unusual Beauty with Serrated Foliage

Proserpinaca palustris, commonly referred to as Mermaid Weed, is a visually striking stem plant celebrated for its finely serrated, almost feathery leaves that shimmer in hues ranging from bright green to deep copper-red depending on lighting and nutrient conditions. This North American native is a favorite among aquascapers looking to add texture, contrast, and a wild, natural touch to planted tanks.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Unique Leaf Shape: Delicate, saw-tooth foliage resembling aquatic ferns or lace adds eye-catching texture to aquascapes.

  • Color Variability: Green under moderate lighting; turns bronze to deep red under intense light and CO₂.

  • Midground to Background Use: Excellent for adding vertical interest and soft movement in flowing water.

  • Native Species: Grows naturally in marshes and slow-moving waters across the U.S., making it an eco-conscious choice.

🔆 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; stronger lighting enhances red tones and leaf serration.

  • CO₂: Highly recommended for best growth and coloration, though not strictly required.

  • Water Parameters:
    pH: 6.0–7.5
    Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)
    Soft to moderately hard water preferred

🪴 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds contrast to smoother-leaved plants like Anubias or Cryptocoryne.

  • Ideal for nature-style and biotope tanks.

  • The intricate leaf texture softens hardscape elements like rocks and driftwood.

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Propagation: Simply cut and replant healthy stems.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended for optimal growth.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes — ideal for paludariums or riparium setups.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 6 loose, freshly cut stems — unrooted and ready to plant directly into your substrate.
  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6 unrooted, fresh-cut stems bundled together with foam and secured with a weight for easy placement.
  • Potted Plants: Approximately 6 unrooted, fresh-cut stems nestled in rockwool and housed in a net pot — ideal for stable planting and easy transfer.

🔒 Disclaimer:

Photos shown are sample representations. Actual plants may vary in size, shape, and coloration depending on growing conditions. Aquatic plants may be shipped emersed or submersed, and while we inspect every shipment, we cannot guarantee plants are 100% free of snails or algae.

Unlike more common Ludwigia species, L. sphaerocarpa exhibits a compact, rosette-like growth habit with thick, horizontally spreading stems that add striking structure and texture to any aquascape. When grown submerged with high light and CO₂, its foliage transitions from bright green to intense orange-red to reddish-brown, offering a warm, vibrant contrast in planted tanks.

Now available in our sterile Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag, this plant is propagated through advanced Tissue Culture Technology—ensuring a 100% pest-free, algae-free, and pesticide-free start. products.title: Anubias Petite White Platinum Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Anubias Petite White Platinum is a rare and exquisite variety of the popular Anubias aquarium plant, renowned for its stunning albino white leaves. Native to Africa, this compact plant adds an elegant touch to any aquascape with its unique coloration and manageable size. Although it is hard to grow and has a very slow growth rate, its unique beauty makes it highly sought after by dedicated aquarists.

Key Features:

  • Albino White Leaves: Features striking white leaves that add a touch of elegance and uniqueness to your aquarium.
  • Compact Size: A petite version of Anubias, perfect for creating intricate and detailed aquascapes.
  • Slow Growth: Grows very slowly and requires careful attention, making it a rewarding challenge for experienced aquarists.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in low to moderate lighting conditions, though it requires stable light levels to maintain its health.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers stable water conditions and is best suited for aquariums maintained by experienced hobbyists.

Planting and Care:

  • Attachment: Can be attached to rocks, driftwood, or other decorations using fishing line or thread.
  • Rhizome Placement: Ensure the rhizome (the thick stem from which leaves emerge) is not buried in the substrate to prevent rot. Attach it to a solid surface or plant it with the rhizome above the substrate.
  • Fertilization and Pruning: Requires minimal fertilizer and occasional pruning to maintain its size and shape. Regular monitoring and stable conditions are key to its survival.

Benefits:

  • Unique Aesthetic: Its rare albino white leaves enhance the visual appeal of any aquarium, providing a striking contrast to green foliage.
  • Hardiness: Despite its slow growth, Anubias White Platinum Petite is known for its durability and can thrive with careful maintenance.
  • Rewarding Challenge: Ideal for experienced aquarists looking for a unique and sophisticated addition to their tank.

Market Rarity:

  • Rare and Elegant: Extremely rare in the market, often priced higher than regular Anubias species due to its scarcity and unique appearance.

Conclusion:

Anubias Petite White Platinum is a stunning and low-maintenance plant that adds a unique and elegant touch to any aquarium. While it is hard to grow and grows very slowly, its rare albino white leaves make it a standout choice for aquarists seeking a rare and sophisticated plant. With careful attention and stable conditions, this plant can become a highlight of your aquatic environment.

Enhance your aquarium with the exquisite beauty of Anubias Petite White Platinum and enjoy the unique transformation it brings to your aquatic world.

products.title: Butterfly Moss
products.body_html:
Butterfly Moss – Rare Aquatic Moss with Wing-Like Leaves

Butterfly Moss (Plagiomnium sp. 'Butterfly') is a rare and captivating liverwort that brings natural beauty and structure to freshwater aquariums. Renowned for its rounded, overlapping leaves that resemble butterfly wings, this moss adds gentle texture and lush greenery to both nature-style and minimalist aquascapes. Despite its delicate appearance, Butterfly Moss is hardy, easy to care for, and suitable for a wide range of tank setups.

Whether you're designing a serene shrimp habitat or a detailed moss wall, Plagiomnium sp. 'Butterfly' is a superb choice for aquarists seeking rare, low-maintenance plants that offer both aesthetics and habitat benefits.

🌿 Key Features of Butterfly Moss

  • Scientific Name: Plagiomnium sp. 'Butterfly'

  • Common Name: Butterfly Moss

  • Leaf Structure: Small, rounded leaves resembling butterfly wings

  • Rhizoids Present: Hairlike structures often mistaken for algae serve as anchoring roots

  • Versatile Use: Attachable to driftwood, stone, or substrate for foreground, midground, or vertical layouts

  • Moderate Growth Rate: Forms lush green mats over time, requiring minimal trimming

✅ Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Low to medium (avoid direct, intense light to prevent drying)

  • CO₂: Not required, though CO₂ supplementation can promote fuller growth

  • Substrate: Not required—best attached to hardscape elements

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–77°F (20–25°C)

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Aesthetic Focal Point: Unique, wing-like foliage provides visual interest in nature aquariums

  • Shelter for Aquatic Life: Offers protective hiding places for shrimp, fry, and small fish

  • Beginner Friendly: Low care requirements and slow, manageable growth

  • Custom Layout Potential: Can be attached to mesh, rocks, driftwood, or left free-floating

🌱 Why Choose Butterfly Moss?

Butterfly Moss is an ideal choice for aquascapers who appreciate delicate structure with strong visual impact. Its elegant leaf pattern, ability to thrive in low-tech setups, and benefits to tank biodiversity make it a favorite for both seasoned and novice hobbyists. Whether used as a moss carpet or a cascading accent, it brings life and balance to any aquascape.

products.title: Hygro Willow (Hygrophila Angustifolia)
products.body_html:
Hygrophila Angustifolia, also known as Hygro Willow. This stunning aquatic plant features long, narrow, willow-like leaves that add a touch of elegance and natural beauty to any underwater landscape. Perfect for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Hygrophila Angustifolia is easy to care for and adaptable to various aquarium conditions.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Green Leaves: Adds a lush, green backdrop to your aquarium.
  • Fast Growth Rate: Quickly fills in the background, creating a dense, natural look.
  • Low Maintenance: Thrives in medium to high light conditions without demanding care.
  • Versatile Planting: Ideal for background placement in any aquascape style, from nature to Dutch aquariums.

Care Instructions:

  • Light Requirement: Medium to high.
  • CO2 Supplementation: Beneficial but not necessary.
  • Optimal Temperature: 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • pH Range: 6.5 to 7.5.

Trimming and Propagation:

  • Trimming: Regular trimming is essential to maintain the plant's shape and prevent it from overshadowing other plants. Use sharp scissors to cut back the stems to the desired height.
  • Propagation: Easily propagate Hygrophila Angustifolia through stem cuttings. Simply cut a healthy stem with several leaves and replant it in the substrate. The new cutting will quickly develop roots and start growing.

Transform your aquarium with the lush greenery of Hygrophila Angustifolia. Order now and enjoy fast, reliable shipping to bring this beautiful plant into your aquatic paradise.

products.title: Amazon Sword Mother Plant
products.body_html:
Amazon Sword (Echinodorus bleheri) is one of the most popular and versatile aquatic plants among aquarists. Known for its lush, broad leaves and vibrant green color, this plant is a perfect choice for creating a natural and aesthetically pleasing underwater landscape.

Key Features:

  • Robust Growth: Amazon Sword is a hardy plant that can thrive in a variety of aquarium conditions. Its robust growth makes it an ideal choice for beginners and experienced aquarists alike.
  • Visual Appeal: With its large, sword-shaped leaves, Amazon Sword adds a dramatic and natural look to any aquarium. It serves as an excellent background plant, providing shelter and hiding spots for fish.
  • Low Maintenance: This plant is easy to care for and requires minimal maintenance. It can adapt to a wide range of lighting conditions, though it thrives best under moderate to high light.

Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Amazon Sword prefers moderate to high lighting to promote healthy growth and vibrant coloration.
  • CO2 and Nutrients: While not strictly necessary, CO2 supplementation and regular fertilization with macro and micronutrients can enhance growth and overall health.
  • Water Parameters: This plant is adaptable to various water conditions but prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH (6.5-7.5) and temperatures between 22-28°C (72-82°F).

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Enhancement: The lush, green foliage of Amazon Sword enhances the visual appeal of any aquarium, creating a natural and serene environment.
  • Habitat Creation: Provides excellent cover and hiding spots for fish, promoting a more secure and natural habitat.
  • Versatility: Suitable for a range of aquascaping styles, from beginner setups to intricate, high-tech aquariums.

Conclusion:

Amazon Sword (Echinodorus bleheri) is a must-have for any aquarium enthusiast. Its robust growth, striking appearance, and ease of care make it an ideal choice for enhancing the beauty and functionality of your aquarium. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced aquarist, Amazon Sword will bring life and vibrancy to your underwater world.

products.title: Dwarf Hairgrass
products.body_html:
Eleocharis pusilla, commonly known as Dwarf Hairgrass in the planted aquarium community, is a grass-like plant that is perfect for creating lush carpet foregrounds in fish tanks and aquariums. This easy-to-care-for plant adds a natural, meadow-like appearance to any aquascape. Eleocharis Pusilla can be compared with the popular Eleocharis Acicularis and Eleocharis Acicularis 'Mini'.

Key Features:

  • Grass-Like Appearance: Features fine, grass-like leaves that form a dense carpet, enhancing the natural look of your aquarium.
  • Small Flower Spike: When grown above water, Eleocharis pusilla produces a very small head or spike of flowers, only a few millimeters long.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions.
  • Water Parameters: Adaptable to a range of freshwater conditions, making it suitable for various aquarium setups.

Propagation:

  • Runners: Easily propagates through runners, quickly creating a thick, green carpet in your aquarium.
  • Growth Rate: Moderate growth rate, allowing for manageable maintenance and a continuously lush appearance.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a beautiful, natural carpet effect to the foreground of your aquarium, enhancing the overall aquascape.
  • Easy Care: Ideal for both beginner and experienced aquarists due to its low maintenance requirements.

Market Information:

  • Trade Name: Eleocharis pusilla has been sold as Eleocharis parvula in the aquarium trade for quite some time.
  • Species Clarification: Most Eleocharis parvula sold in the market is actually Eleocharis pusilla, as Eleocharis parvula is a brackish water species not suitable for freshwater aquariums.

Conclusion:

Eleocharis pusilla (Dwarf Hairgrass) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a lush, grass-like carpet to their freshwater aquariums. Its easy propagation through runners and ability to thrive in various conditions make it a favorite among the aquarium community. By choosing Eleocharis pusilla, you ensure a vibrant, natural aquascape that enhances the beauty of your underwater environment.

Transform your aquarium with the elegant and natural beauty of Eleocharis pusilla and enjoy a thriving, verdant aquascape.

products.title: Bucephalandra King Blue Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra sp. King Blue is a captivating and rare aquatic plant that brings a splash of striking color to any aquarium. This unique variety is prized for its deep blue-green leaves and compact growth, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts who seek to create a captivating and colorful underwater landscape.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. 'King Blue'
  • Common Name: Bucephalandra King Blue
  • Family: Araceae
  • Leaf Color: Deep blue-green
  • Growth Form: Compact, rhizome-based

Unique Characteristics
Bucephalandra King Blue is distinguished by its stunning blue-green leaves and compact size. This rare and exquisite plant adds a unique visual element to aquariums, providing contrast and depth. Its slow growth rate and small stature make it perfect for nano tanks and intricate aquascapes.

Ideal Conditions
To ensure optimal growth and vibrant color for Bucephalandra King Blue, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Low to moderate intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Can be attached to rocks or driftwood
  • Water Temperature: 22-28°C (72-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance
Bucephalandra King Blue is relatively easy to care for and suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Low to moderate light levels help maintain its vibrant blue-green coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports healthy growth and overall health.
  • Trimming: Minimal trimming is needed; remove any damaged or algae-covered leaves.

Aquascaping Uses
Bucephalandra King Blue is versatile and perfect for various aquascaping applications:

  • Foreground Plant: Ideal for creating a colorful and detailed foreground.
  • Midground Plant: Adds a unique blue-green accent to the midground area.
  • Accent Plant: Its vibrant blue-green leaves make it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The deep blue-green leaves of Bucephalandra King Blue provide a striking contrast against the green foliage typically found in aquariums, making it a standout choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation
Propagation of Bucephalandra King Blue is straightforward. This plant propagates through rhizome division. Simply cut a healthy rhizome section with leaves and roots, and reattach it to a new location in the aquarium.

Sustainable and Ethical Practices
Bucephalandra takes a very long time to develop into a thick, compact carpet over rocks and wood in nature. Due to the popularity of the Bucephalandra species, they have been heavily harvested from the wild, putting them at risk of extinction. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we are committed to providing farm-raised and tissue culture Bucephalandra species to save forests and protect the natural rivers and rainforests of Indonesia. By choosing our plants, you are supporting sustainable and ethical aquascaping practices.

Bucephalandra King Blue is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a rare and visually striking plant. Its vibrant blue-green color, compact growth, and ease of care make it a standout addition to any underwater landscape.

products.title: Nymphaea August Koch | Violet-Blue Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
Nymphaea August Koch is a hybrid water lily renowned for its large violet-blue flowers and rich history. Named after August Koch, a German water lily hybridizer who, along with George H. Pring, created many new cultivars in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. This tropical lily, introduced in 1922, stands out for its stunning blooms and easy-care requirements, making it a popular choice among water gardeners.

Key Features:

  • Violet-Blue Flowers: Produces large, vibrant violet-blue flowers that are both beautiful and fragrant.
  • Olive Green Pads: Features olive green leaves that provide a lush backdrop for the striking flowers.
  • Historical Significance: Named after August Koch, the hybrid was created in 1922 at Garfield Park, Chicago, Illinois, and is highly viviparous.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Thrives in full sun, requiring at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily.
  • Water Temperature: Prefers water temperatures between 70°F and 80°F (21°C to 27°C).
  • Planting Depth: Ideal for ponds or containers with still water.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with water lily fertilizer tablets is necessary to ensure healthy growth and blooming. Add tablets to the soil every few weeks during the growing season.
  • Pruning: Remove dead leaves and flowers occasionally to maintain plant health and encourage new growth.
  • Winter Care: In cooler climates, it is best to lift the plant from the container and store the rhizome in damp sand and peat moss at a temperature of 50-55°F (10-12°C).

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Enhances the visual appeal of any pond or water garden with its stunning violet-blue flowers and lush green foliage.
  • Wildlife Attraction: Attracts pollinators such as bees and butterflies, contributing to a healthy ecosystem.
  • Fragrance: The flowers emit a pleasant fragrance, enhancing the sensory experience of your garden.

Historical and Botanical Notes:

  • Parentage: The parentage is likely Nymphaea ‘Blue Beauty’ x Nymphaea ‘Mrs. Woodrow Wilson’.
  • Creation: Developed by August Koch and George H. Pring, notable water lily hybridizers of their time.

Conclusion:

Nymphaea August Koch is a spectacular hybrid water lily that brings vibrant color and historical charm to any water garden or pond. Its robust nature, striking violet-blue flowers, and ease of care make it an excellent choice for both novice and experienced gardeners. Enhance your aquatic landscape with the exquisite beauty of Nymphaea August Koch and enjoy the vibrant transformation it brings to your environment.

    • You will get 1 young and freshly harvested water lily tuber
    • All of our water lilies tubers/rhizomes have been inspected before ship out and guaranteed they are viable and perfect to grow in a proper pond environment

    PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

     

    There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

Gently rinse with aquarium water or dechlorinated water before planting.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Balansae
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne crispatula var. balansae is a lovely plant native to southern Thailand. Depending on the environment, the leaves are either a lush green or a bronze tint with a ruffled, wavy texture.

The Cryptocoryne crispatula var. balansae plant has little maintenance. It thrives in hard water with a high calcium concentration and enjoys moderate to bright light. It requires a good fertilization regimen, which may be complemented with a rich substrate fertilizer, for the best growth. Once established, the plant grows quickly and requires minimal care.

Anubias Congensis Mini is a refined, compact variety of the classic Anubias congensis, celebrated for its narrow, lance-shaped leaves and slow, steady growth. Perfect for foreground or midground placement, this miniaturized version is ideal for nano tanks or detailed aquascapes where space is limited but lush greenery is desired. Its hardy nature and low-maintenance requirements make it a favorite for both beginners and seasoned aquascapers.

products.title: Dwarf Hairgrass Mini
products.body_html:
Eleocharis acicularis 'Mini', also known as Eleocharis sp. 'Mini', Dwarf Hairgrass 'Mini', Eleocharis sp. Belem, or Japanese Hairgrass, is a highly sought-after aquatic plant for creating stunning foreground carpets in aquariums. This variant of hairgrass stays exceptionally compact and short, reaching approximately 3-5 cm in height, making it the shortest growing hairgrass available.

Key Features:

  • Compact Growth: Stays short at approximately 3-5 cm, making it ideal for creating dense, lush carpets.
  • Unique Leaf Shape: Leaves tend to curl downward when grown in very high light, CO2, and nutrient-rich environments.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in very high light conditions, which encourages the compact and curling leaf growth.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers nutrient-rich environments and benefits from CO2 supplementation.

Propagation:

  • Runners: Easily propagates via runners, allowing it to quickly spread and form a dense, lush lawn.
  • Growth Rate: Fast-growing under optimal conditions, making it perfect for creating a vibrant carpet.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a beautiful, natural carpet effect to the foreground of your aquarium, enhancing the overall aquascape design.
  • Low Maintenance: Its compact growth and easy propagation make it suitable for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

Comparison:

  • Shortest Hairgrass: Currently the shortest growing hairgrass (3-5 cm), even smaller than the popular Eleocharis pusilla (Parvula).

Conclusion:

Eleocharis acicularis 'Mini' (Dwarf Hairgrass 'Mini') is an excellent choice for aquarists seeking to create a stunning, dense carpet in their aquariums. Its compact size, unique leaf shape, and ease of propagation make it a standout addition to any aquascape. Whether you're aiming for a lush lawn or a detailed foreground, Eleocharis acicularis 'Mini' provides the perfect solution for your aquascaping needs.

Transform your aquarium with the elegant and compact beauty of Eleocharis acicularis 'Mini' and enjoy a vibrant, natural underwater landscape.

products.title: Java Fern Gold Sparkle
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Gold Sparkle’ – Golden-Leaf Java Fern with a Subtle Shimmer

Microsorum pteropus ‘Gold Sparkle’, also known as Java Fern Gold Sparkle, is a captivating and rare variety of Java Fern that features delicate gold-tinted leaves with a subtle sparkling effect. Its unique coloration and fine texture add a soft golden glow to any freshwater aquascape, offering both visual contrast and elegance without overwhelming the layout.

Native to Southeast Asia, this variety thrives in low to medium lighting and does not require CO₂ supplementation, making it ideal for low-tech aquariums or beginner hobbyists. Like other Microsorum species, Gold Sparkle should be attached to driftwood, rocks, or decor—never buried in substrate, as its rhizome is prone to rot if covered. It grows slowly, requiring minimal maintenance beyond occasional trimming to manage legginess or shape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate this plant in its emersed form. Once introduced into your tank, it transitions well to submerged conditions, adding shimmering texture to your midground or background zones.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Golden Shimmering Leaves: Unique gold-colored foliage with a soft sparkle under aquarium lighting

  • Attachable Growth: Best mounted on hardscape—no need for substrate planting

  • Low Maintenance: Perfect for low-tech setups; grows well in low to medium light without CO₂

  • Slow-Growing: Minimal trimming required and easy to manage

  • Versatile Placement: Adds refined texture and contrast to midground or background areas

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus ‘Gold Sparkle’

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus 'Gold Sparkle’

  • Common Names: Java Fern Gold Sparkle

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or hardscape

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

  • Origin: Southeast Asia

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Prefers low to medium light; too much light may cause leaf discoloration

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: CO₂ is not required; occasional liquid fertilization supports long-term health

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation Method: Rhizome division

Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

products.title: Java Fern Coral Reef
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Coral Reef’ – Rare Split-Leaf Java Fern

Microsorum pteropus 'Coral Reef', also known as Java Fern Coral, is an exciting and rare cultivar of Java Fern that brings a wild and unpredictable charm to planted aquariums. This variety features randomly split, fishtail-like leaves—a trait that sets it apart from more predictable variants like Microsorum 'Trident' and Windelov. Each leaf can split in an unexpected pattern, giving every plant a unique and organic appearance.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we grow Java Fern Coral Reef emersed in our nursery, where its leaf coloration ranges from light green to deep green. Under aquarium conditions, especially with strong lighting, new growth may display hints of pink or light red at the leaf tips—adding delicate color highlights to your aquascape.

As with other Microsorum species, Java Fern Coral Reef does not require planting in substrate. Instead, it thrives when attached to driftwood, rocks, or other decor, making it a versatile option for creative aquascaping layouts. It’s a slow-growing and low-maintenance plant, perfect for both beginners and advanced aquascapers seeking rare botanical accents.

🌟 Key Features:

products.title: Helanthium Quadricostatus (Broad Leaf Chain Sword)
products.body_html:
Helanthium Quadricostatus, also known as Broad Leaf Chain Sword, is a species of aquatic plant that belongs to the Alismataceae family. It is native to South America, where it is found in the Amazon River Basin and the Rio Paraguay.

The plant is popular among aquarium enthusiasts due to its attractive appearance and easy care. It has broad leaves that grow in a rosette at the base of the plant, and the leaves can grow up to 10 inches long and 1 inch wide. The plant can reach a height of up to 20 inches, and it produces runners that can spread the plant throughout the aquarium.

The Broad Leaf Chain Sword is a hardy plant that can tolerate a wide range of water conditions, including low to moderate lighting and pH levels between 6.5 and 7.5. It prefers soft to medium-hard water, and it can be propagated by separating the plant's runners.

In an aquarium setting, Helanthium Quadricostatus provides excellent cover for fish and can help reduce the levels of nitrates and other pollutants in the water. It is also commonly used in aquascaping due to its striking appearance and ability to create a natural-looking aquatic environment.

  • Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants or potted plants

Disclaimer: Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation. Unless specific, we don't guarantee aquatic plants will be free of pests, and pesticides, grow emerged, or submerge.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Albida Red
products.body_html:
🌿 Cryptocoryne Albida ‘Red’ – Rare, CO₂-Dependent Crypt with Reddish Hue

Sold as Bare-Root, Rooted Bunched, or Rooted Potted Plants | For Experienced Aquascapers

Cryptocoryne Albida ‘Red’ is a rare and striking foreground crypt that stands out for its deep bronze-to-reddish coloration and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. Unlike common Crypt species, this plant requires a stable, enriched environment with pressurized CO₂ injection, nutrient-rich substrate, and consistent care. It is best suited for experienced aquascapers looking to challenge their skills with a demanding but visually rewarding species.

Once established, it forms a dense rosette and adds earthy depth and texture to any layout.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Cryptocoryne albida ‘Red’

  • Common Name: Crypt Albida Red

  • Leaf Shape: Narrow, lanceolate

  • Leaf Color: Reddish-bronze to dark olive

  • Growth Form: Compact rosette

  • Ideal Placement: Foreground to midground

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Light Requirement: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: ✅ Required

  • Nutrient Substrate: ✅ Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: ✅ Yes (with stable humidity and light)

🪴 What You’ll Receive

  • Bare-Root Plants: 1–2 small, loose, unpotted plants

  • Rooted Bunched Plants: 1–2 plants bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Rooted Potted Plants: 1–2 plants rooted in rockwool and net pot

  • This listing features young plants grown in a controlled laboratory environment (ex-vitro), offering a clean, pest-free start ideal for experienced aquascapers ready to meet its advanced care requirements.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • A rare collector’s crypt with subtle red tones

  • Excellent for filling low zones with rich, natural texture

  • Contrasts beautifully with green carpeting or moss species

  • Stays compact without overcrowding small tanks

⚠️ Disclaimer

Cryptocoryne Albida ‘Red’ is a delicate and sensitive species that may undergo "Crypt melt"—a natural shedding of leaves—when transitioning to a new environment. This response is common and temporary. To ensure successful growth, it is essential to maintain stable water parameters, provide pressurized CO₂, and use a nutrient-rich substrate.

products.title: Echinodorus Uruguayensis
products.body_html:
🌿 Aquarius uruguayensis – Lush, Broad-Leaf Background Sword

Aquarius uruguayensis, once classified as Echinodorus uruguayensis, is a bold and elegant sword plant cherished for its broad, arching leaves and impressive size. Native to South America (Uruguay, Brazil, and Argentina), this aquatic plant has long been a favorite among aquascapers for creating a dense, vibrant green backdrop in freshwater aquariums.

With the ability to grow leaves up to 12 inches (30 cm) long and 4 inches (10 cm) wide, this species makes a dramatic visual statement. Whether used as a single focal piece or grouped to create a lush aquascape, Aquarius uruguayensis delivers natural beauty with its rich coloration and flowing leaf structure.

🌟 Key Features

  • Synonyms: Echinodorus uruguayensis, Echinodorus africanus

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required, but beneficial

  • Lighting: Medium to High

  • Leaf Size: Up to 12" long and 4" wide

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🌱 Growth & Propagation

This plant thrives best in aquariums with a nutrient-rich substrate and ample light. While CO₂ is not necessary, its addition can encourage more robust and vibrant growth. Propagation is typically achieved through adventitious plantlets that develop along flowering stalks—these can be removed and replanted to start new specimens.

  • Provides natural hiding spaces for fish and shrimp

  • Pairs beautifully with driftwood, stones, and carpeting plants

products.title: Lobelia Cardinalis Mini
products.body_html:
🌿 Lobelia cardinalis 'Mini' – Compact Foreground Lobelia with Vibrant Green Leaves

Lobelia cardinalis 'Mini' is a dwarf variant of the popular Cardinal Plant, selectively bred for its smaller leaf size and compact growth habit. This cultivar features crisp, bright green foliage that grows densely and horizontally, making it ideal for foreground or midground placement in aquascaped tanks. With regular trimming and high light, this plant forms low, bushy clusters that create a clean, manicured appearance.

Unlike its larger cousin, which often reaches the background zone, Lobelia cardinalis 'Mini' stays neat and controlled. Its smaller internodal spacing and vibrant coloration make it a favorite among aquascapers seeking balance and contrast between stem plants and carpets.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Lobelia cardinalis 'Mini'

  • Common Name: Dwarf Cardinal Plant, Mini Cardinalis

  • Compact Form: Grows low and horizontal with small rounded leaves

  • Bright Color: Displays vibrant, fresh green foliage under moderate to high light

  • Clean Structure: Excellent for structured layouts and Dutch-style aquascapes

  • Perfect for Foreground/Midground: Offers layering and visual balance

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ injection recommended for compact growth and vibrant coloration

  • Growth Method: Submerged

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes (emerges well in humid paludariums and wabi-kusa setups)

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • pH Range: 6.0 – 7.5

  • Temperature Tolerance: 64°F – 82°F (18°C – 28°C)

  • Propagation: Trim and replant stem cuttings; side shoots may also root

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Ideal for creating crisp rows or defined patches in the foreground

  • Adds vibrant green contrast next to red or bronze stem plants

  • Offers clean geometry and control for Dutch and nature-style tanks

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 3 individual, loose-cut stems without root

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 3 stems without root bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 3 stems without root potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

As a high-light, nutrient-loving stem plant, Lobelia cardinalis 'Mini' requires a stable environment with consistent fertilization and CO₂ for optimal form. Occasional trimming helps maintain a low and bushy profile. Some leaf melt may occur during early transition from emersed to submerged growth but should stabilize quickly.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please do not release this plant into natural waterways. Always follow local laws and protect native ecosystems by disposing of trimmings responsibly.

products.title: Bucephalandra Mini Boyan
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra ‘Mini Boyan’

A Tiny Jewel from Borneo for Nano and Midground Aquascapes

Bucephalandra ‘Mini Boyan’ is a petite, slow-growing epiphyte native to the riverbanks of Borneo. This particular variety is known for its compact, oval-shaped leaves and its glossy, deep green to bluish-green coloration, often with subtle iridescence under aquarium lighting. Perfectly sized for nano tanks or detailed midground scaping, this miniature form adds texture and elegance without overwhelming your layout.

Whether you're creating a high-end Iwagumi scape or a forest-style aquascape, Mini Boyan delivers a premium aesthetic while remaining low-maintenance and undemanding.

🌿 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Mini Boyan’

  • Leaf Shape & Size: Tiny oval leaves, approx. 0.3–0.5 inches

  • Coloration: Deep green with bluish or purple sheen under high light

  • Growth Form: Compact clump, rhizomatous

  • Placement: Foreground, midground, or hardscape attachment

  • Growth Rate: Very slow

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Low to medium; intense light enhances color and sheen

  • CO₂: Not required, but improves growth and vibrancy

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0 – 7.5

    • Temperature: 68°F – 78°F (20°C – 26°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderate

  • Flow: Mild to moderate; good water circulation helps prevent algae buildup on leaves

🌱 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Compact size perfect for nano tanks or detailed hardscape accents

  • Great for attaching to driftwood, rocks, or bonsai roots

  • Provides lush, natural contrast against mosses or ferns

  • Adds visual interest with its iridescent texture and dark tone

✂️ Propagation & Growth

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (epiphyte – do not bury rhizome)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

  • Propagated by gently cutting the rhizome between leaf nodes. Attach new segments to hardscape using super glue or thread for best results.

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • Loose Bare-Root Plants: Each order includes one Bucephalandra ‘Mini Boyan’ clump with 4+ healthy leaves, ready to attach to wood or stone.

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner


Please ensure Bucephalandra is not restricted in your area before purchasing. Always follow local guidelines when disposing of plant material.

📢 Disclaimer

As a naturally slow grower, Bucephalandra ‘Mini Boyan’ requires patience and stable conditions. Avoid burying the rhizome to prevent rot, and provide good circulation to keep leaves algae-free. Over time, this miniature jewel will reward you with dense, healthy clumps and unique texture.

products.title: Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini is an exquisite and rare aquatic plant that adds a touch of elegance and sophistication to any aquarium. Known for its compact size and vibrant colors, this plant is ideal for aquarists looking to enhance their aquascape with a unique and eye-catching element. Perfect for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini offers easy care and adaptability, making it a must-have for any planted tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini

  1. Compact Size: Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini is known for its small, compact growth, making it perfect for nano tanks and foreground placement in larger aquariums. Its petite size allows for creative aquascaping and detailed underwater landscapes.

  2. Vibrant Colors: This plant boasts dark green to bluish-green leaves with a subtle iridescent sheen. The unique coloration adds a stunning visual appeal, creating a captivating focal point in your aquarium.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini is highly adaptable and thrives in a range of water conditions. It is resilient and can tolerate low to moderate lighting, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. This natural growth habit makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its natural environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The vibrant, iridescent leaves of Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini add a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Kedagang Mini is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Bucephalandra Belindae
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Belindae (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.

products.title: Echinodorus Big Bear
products.body_html:
Introducing Echinodorus 'Big Bear,' a captivating and enduring red-leaved variety that has captivated aquarists since its commercial availability in 1995. Renowned for its popularity, this plant forms a compact rosette, showcasing a stunning array of hues ranging from rusty red to deep, velvety crimson. With maturity, it can reach a height of up to 50 cm (approximately 20 inches), adding a striking presence to any aquarium.

A fascinating aspect of Echinodorus 'Big Bear' lies in its distinct leaf characteristics between its emersed and submersed growth forms. When nurtured in a greenhouse or above water, the plant flourishes with robust green foliage. However, when submerged in an aquarium, its leaves undergo an enchanting metamorphosis, transitioning to vibrant shades of red within just a few days. This transformation never ceases to astound and inspire aquarium enthusiasts.

To ensure the well-being of Echinodorus 'Big Bear' in an aquatic environment, proper care and attention are vital. Adequate lighting, nutrient supplementation, and a suitable substrate all play integral roles in supporting its healthy growth and enhancing the intensity of its mesmerizing colors.

Experience the wonder of Echinodorus 'Big Bear' as it brings a perpetual sense of enchantment to your aquarium, showcasing its ever-changing display of vivid red foliage.

products.title: Nymphaea Maroon Pink | Hardy Water Lily
products.body_html:
🌸 Nymphaea Maroon Pink – Hardy Water Lily with Rich Maroon-Pink Blooms

Nymphaea Maroon Pink is a striking hardy water lily variety that boasts deep maroon-pink petals and a bold, elegant structure. Its full, star-shaped flowers rise above round, leathery green pads, creating a dramatic contrast that enhances the beauty of any pond or water garden. Known for its reliable blooming and cold-hardiness, this variety is an ideal choice for both novice and experienced pond keepers.

Blooming prolifically from late spring through early fall, Nymphaea Maroon Pink brings vivid color and life to outdoor water features. Its flowers open during the day and close in the evening, providing a dynamic, ever-changing display. This lily not only adds aesthetic appeal but also offers shade and shelter for fish and wildlife, contributing to a healthier aquatic environment.

✨ Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Nymphaea 'Maroon Pink'

  • Common Name: Maroon Pink Hardy Water Lily

  • Bloom Color: Deep maroon-pink with layered petals

  • Bloom Size: 4–6 inches across

  • Leaf Color: Green

  • Light Requirement: Full sun for best flowering

  • Hardiness Zone: USDA Zones 4–11

  • Growth Habit: Free-flowering with surface-spanning pads

  • Fragrance: Mildly fragrant blooms

🌱 Ideal For:

  • Outdoor ponds, koi ponds, and water gardens

  • Medium to large containers and tubs

  • Natural wildlife habitats and aquatic landscaping

  • Aquatic hobbyists seeking cold-hardy and colorful lilies

🔁 Planting & Care:

  • Planting Depth: 6–24 inches (from crown to water surface)

  • Planting Medium: Heavy clay loam or aquatic plant soil in a wide container

  • Fertilization: Monthly during growing season for abundant blooms

  • Winter Care: Overwinters in pond bottoms in zones 4–11 without removal

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • Sold as one live tuber with a new young leaf to ensure viability and active growth.

  • Carefully packaged to preserve freshness and viability

⚠️ Disclaimer:

All water lilies are shipped bare-root (as tubers) and should be planted immediately upon arrival for best results. Bloom and leaf color, size, and shape may vary slightly depending on lighting, water quality, and plant maturity.


 

🌿 Planting & Care Guide for Water Garden Plants

Create a thriving, beautiful pond environment by following these easy planting and care steps. For best results, we recommend planting your water lilies in containers rather than directly in the pond bed. This allows for easier maintenance, relocation, and seasonal care.

All water lilies thrive with 8–10 hours of direct sunlight daily and benefit from regular fertilization throughout the growing season.

🌸 Tropical Water Lilies (Day & Night Blooming)

Tropical water lilies are vibrant and rewarding bloomers that thrive in warm water.

  • Container Size: Use a pot 12 inches or larger (3–5 gallons recommended).

  • Soil: Fill halfway with rich garden soil. Add 2–4 fertilizer tablets, then top off with more soil—leaving about 2 inches of space from the rim.

  • Planting: Place the rhizome upright, gently covering the roots while keeping the growing tip above the soil.

  • Top Layer: Add 1–2 inches of pea gravel or sand to prevent soil from floating. Keep gravel away from the crown.

  • Water Depth: Submerge the pot to about 12 inches.

  • Temperature: Wait until the water reaches 70°F (21°C) before planting to prevent dormancy.

  • Blooming Season: Late spring through early fall.

🪷 Tip: A larger pot will encourage stronger growth and more abundant blooms.

❄️ Hardy Water Lilies

Hardy lilies grow horizontally and return each year with proper care.

  • Container Size: Use a wide, shallow container to accommodate horizontal rhizome growth.

  • Planting: Position the rhizome at a 45° angle, near the edge of the pot, with the crown exposed.

  • Soil & Fertilizer: Use nutrient-rich soil and 2–4 fertilizer tablets. Cover the top with 1–2 inches of pea gravel or sand.

  • Water Depth: Begin with 6 inches of water above the soil and gradually lower to 12–18 inches as the plant matures.

  • Planting Season: Early spring.

  • Blooming Season: June through September.

  • Dormancy: Becomes dormant in winter and regrows in spring.

🌞 Quick Care Tips:

  • Use heavy garden soil, not potting mix.

  • Avoid burying the crown of lilies.

  • Fertilize every 3–4 weeks during active growth.

  • Regularly remove yellowing leaves and spent blooms.

featured_image.alt: Close-up of a healthy hardy water lily rhizome showing new leaf growth, held over a pond background with floating pads.

  • Temperature Range: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to moderate

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (feeds from the water column)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🧪 Vitro Culture Advantages:

  • 100% free from algae, snails, and harmful pathogens

  • Grown in sterile lab conditions for maximum purity

  • Safe for shrimp and sensitive aquariums

🌿 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Ideal for layering in backgrounds and side zones

✂️ Propagation & Care Tips:

products.title: Nymphaea Siam Fantasy | Yellow + Red Hardy Water Lily
products.body_html:
Nymphaea ‘Siam Fantasy’ – Exotic Marbled Yellow & Red Hardy Water Lily

Nymphaea ‘Siam Fantasy’ is a breathtaking hardy water lily prized for its bold marbled coloration and generous yellow hues. With a visual appeal that draws comparisons to Nymphaea ‘Wanvisa’, this cultivar stands out with its brighter yellow base petals accented by rich red streaks, creating a tropical, sun-drenched effect on the water’s surface.

Each bloom floats gracefully above the water, opening fully during the day to reveal a striking golden-yellow center. Paired with green lily pads speckled in purple-brown, this variety adds vibrant contrast and movement to any pond or container water garden.

🌼 Key Features:

  • Type: Hardy Water Lily

  • Coloration: Marbled yellow petals with red streaks

  • Bloom Size: Medium to large (4–6 inches)

  • Blooming Season: Late spring to early fall

  • Fragrance: Mildly fragrant

  • Leaf Type: Green with maroon mottling

  • Ideal for: Small to large ponds, containers, water gardens

  • Growth Habit: Free-flowering with consistent blooms in warm seasons

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Full sun (at least 6 hours/day)

  • Water Depth: 12 to 24 inches from crown to surface

  • Hardiness Zones: USDA Zones 4–10

  • Fertilization: Monthly with aquatic plant fertilizer during growing season

  • Water Temperature: 70–85°F (21–29°C)

🌿 Aquascaping & Pond Benefits:

  • Enhances ponds with vivid, bi-colored blooms that draw the eye

  • Provides natural shade and cooling for fish and aquatic life

  • Helps reduce algae by limiting light penetration

  • Adds visual interest with contrasting lily pads

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • One young, healthy, field-grown bare-root Nymphaea ‘Siam Fantasy’ rhizome

  • Carefully packaged to preserve freshness and viability

⚠️ Disclaimer:
Please allow 1–2 growing seasons for your lily to reach its full blooming potential. Flower shape and coloration may vary slightly depending on growing conditions, lighting, and water quality.



PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

 

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

featured_image.alt: Close-up of a healthy hardy water lily rhizome showing new leaf growth, held over a pond background with floating pads.

products.title: Java Fern Lionfish
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Lionfish’ – Exotic Java Fern with Fin-Like Fronds

Microsorum pteropus ‘Lionfish’, also known as Java Fern Lionfish, is an exotic and captivating cultivar of the classic Java Fern that brings a bold, ornamental flair to freshwater aquascapes. Named for its elegant, finely dissected leaves that resemble the fins of a lionfish, this variety displays a dramatic texture and silhouette that stands out in any aquarium layout.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we grow this plant emersed in our nursery to ensure strong, healthy starts. Once transitioned to submerged growth, Lionfish flourishes in low- to moderate-light tanks and develops into a stunning focal plant. Its deep green, ribboned foliage not only enhances visual depth but also offers shelter and refuge for shrimp, fry, and small fish.

Java Fern Lionfish is a rhizome plant and should be attached to rocks, driftwood, or decor rather than planted in substrate. Its slow growth rate, combined with its adaptability and low care demands, makes it an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists seeking a distinctive botanical accent.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Striking Leaf Structure: Finely divided, serrated leaves resemble lionfish fins

  • Deep Green Coloration: Adds bold contrast to fish and aquatic plants

  • Attachable Rhizome Plant: Best mounted on stone, wood, or decor—no soil needed

  • Low Maintenance: Thrives in low to medium lighting without CO₂

  • Excellent Shelter: Dense foliage provides hiding spots for shrimp and small fish

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus ‘Lionfish’

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus ‘Lionfish’

  • Common Names: Java Fern Lionfish

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or hardscape

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

  • Origin: Southeast Asia

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Low to medium light—enhances leaf detail without promoting algae

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: CO₂ is not required; occasional fertilization supports healthy growth

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

    • Can tolerate slightly brackish conditions

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation Method: Rhizome division

products.title: Anubias Hastifolia
products.body_html:
Anubias hastifolia is a stunning, broad-leaved aquatic plant known for its unique arrowhead-shaped foliage and adaptability in a wide range of freshwater aquarium setups. Native to West Africa, this hardy and undemanding species is prized for its ability to thrive in both submerged and emersed environments, making it a versatile and long-lasting addition to any aquascape. Its impressive size and lush green leaves make it a striking background or centerpiece plant in large aquariums.

Key Features & Benefits

  • Distinctive Arrowhead Leaves: Features large, hastate (spear-shaped) leaves with a rich green hue that adds structure and elegance to aquascapes

  • Hardy & Low Maintenance: Grows slowly and requires minimal care—ideal for beginners and low-tech setups

  • Low Light Compatible: Thrives in low to moderate lighting conditions; does not require CO₂ injection

  • Versatile Placement: Can be used as a background plant or tied to driftwood and rocks for vertical dimension

  • Easy Propagation: Propagates via rhizome division; do not bury the rhizome to prevent rot

  • Compatible with a Wide Range of Setups: Great for community tanks, cichlid tanks, and paludariums

Why Choose Anubias Hastifolia?

Aquarists searching for a hardy and decorative plant that doesn’t demand high light or specialized care will find a perfect match in Anubias hastifolia. Its bold, arrow-shaped leaves provide dramatic foliage contrast while improving water quality by aiding in natural nutrient absorption. This plant is a favorite for aquarists designing large or heavily planted tanks where visual impact and ease of care are equally important. Its robust nature also makes it resistant to most herbivorous fish, offering long-lasting beauty in a wide variety of freshwater environments. Whether you're setting up a lush jungle-style tank or a clean, modern aquascape, Anubias hastifolia delivers unmatched durability and aesthetic appeal.

products.title: Echinodorus grisebachii 'Small'
products.body_html:
Echinodorus grisebachii 'Small' – Compact Elegance for Planted Aquariums

Echinodorus grisebachii 'Small' is a compact version of the classic Amazon Sword, offering all the beauty of its larger cousin in a smaller, more versatile size. Ideal for nano tanks and midground placement, this elegant sword plant features slender green leaves that provide rich texture without overwhelming your aquascape. Whether you're designing a low-maintenance community tank or a detailed nature aquarium, this plant adds a lush, balanced look while being easy to care for.

Key Features, Benefits & Growth Tips

  • 🌿 Compact Growth: Stays smaller than traditional Amazon Swords—perfect for nano tanks or midground planting in larger layouts.

  • 🌱 Low-Maintenance: Thrives in low to moderate light and adapts well to a wide range of water conditions.

  • ✂️ Propagation: Reproduces through runners that grow baby plants—simply trim and replant to expand coverage.

  • 💧 Best Results: Use nutrient-rich substrate and root tabs for faster, fuller growth. CO₂ is optional but enhances leaf density.

  • 🐠 Aquatic Life Friendly: Offers natural cover for fish and shrimp while improving oxygenation and tank stability.

  • 🧑🌾 Ideal For: Midground in large tanks, background in nano tanks, low-tech planted setups, and biotope aquariums.

Why Choose Echinodorus grisebachii 'Small'?

Echinodorus grisebachii 'Small' solves a common problem for aquascapers: finding a sword plant that fits in smaller tanks or tighter spaces without sacrificing beauty. Unlike the full-sized Amazon Sword, this variety remains compact while still offering the signature sword-shaped leaves and rich green coloration. It’s an excellent option for aquarists who want to create depth and volume in their fish tank without constant trimming or overpowering other plants. Hardy and reliable, it's a perfect addition for both beginners and seasoned plant keepers alike.

products.title: Echinodorus Amazonicus
products.body_html:
Echinodorus Amazonicus (Amazon Sword) is a classic freshwater aquarium plant celebrated for its lush, vibrant green foliage and ease of care. While often compared to Echinodorus Bleherae, another popular plant commonly referred to as the Amazon Sword, there are notable distinctions between the two, making each suited to specific aquascaping needs.

Key Features:

  • Lush Green Foliage: Features long, narrow leaves that are slightly more streamlined compared to the broader leaves of Echinodorus Bleherae, giving a sleek and elegant appearance.
  • Size and Growth Habit: Typically grows up to 16–20 inches tall, slightly smaller than Echinodorus Bleherae, which can reach up to 24 inches, making it ideal for medium-sized tanks.
  • Versatility in Aquascaping: Serves as an excellent background or centerpiece plant, particularly in smaller tanks where Echinodorus Bleherae might feel overwhelming.

Comparison to Echinodorus Bleherae:

  • Leaf Shape: Echinodorus Amazonicus features more elongated and narrower leaves, whereas Echinodorus Bleherae has broader, paddle-shaped leaves.
  • Size: Echinodorus Bleherae generally grows larger, making it better suited for very large tanks, while Echinodorus Amazonicus is more versatile for medium to large setups.
  • Lighting Needs: Both species thrive in low to medium lighting but will exhibit more robust growth and coloration under higher lighting conditions.
  • Care: Both plants are hardy and beginner-friendly, with similar nutrient and substrate requirements.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting encourages faster growth and deeper green hues.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH (6.5–7.5) and temperatures of 72°F–82°F (22°C–28°C).
  • Substrate: Thrives in nutrient-rich substrates and benefits from root tabs in standard setups.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Habitat: Provides shade and shelter, mimicking natural environments for fish and shrimp.
  • Oxygenation: Helps oxygenate the tank and absorb excess nutrients, improving overall water quality.
  • Aesthetic Impact: Enhances aquascapes with its striking greenery and fills empty spaces with its bushy, upright growth.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Low Maintenance: Like Echinodorus Bleherae, it is easy to care for and resilient to varying conditions.
  • Propagation: Spreads through runners, allowing for natural growth over time.
  • Trimming: Periodic trimming of older leaves ensures the plant remains healthy and visually appealing.

Conclusion:

While Echinodorus Amazonicus and Echinodorus Bleherae share similarities as members of the Amazon Sword family, their differences in size, leaf shape, and aesthetic make them unique choices for aquariums. Echinodorus Amazonicus is perfect for medium to large tanks, offering lush greenery without the overwhelming size of its larger counterpart. Whether you choose one or both, these iconic plants will transform your tank into a vibrant, thriving aquatic landscape.

products.title: Hydrocotyle Verticillata (Umbrella Pennywort)
products.body_html:
Hydrocotyle verticillata, also known as Whorled Pennywort, Whorled Marshpennywort, Shield Pennywort, and Umbrella Pennywort, is a versatile and attractive plant that stands out for its unique trailing growth form. Unlike other Hydrocotyle species, this plant retains its distinctive whorled leaves and trailing habit, making it a favorite among aquarists and gardeners alike.

Key Features:

  • Trailing Growth: Unlike other Hydrocotyle species, Hydrocotyle verticillata retains its trailing growth form, adding a unique aesthetic to any setting.
  • Distinctive Leaves: Features round, whorled leaves that resemble small umbrellas or shields, providing a lush and green appearance.

Versatile Applications:

  • Garden Pond: Ideal for garden ponds, where it can float on the surface or trail along the edges, adding a natural touch to water features.
  • Aquarium Use: Perfect for aquariums, either planted in the substrate or used as a floating plant, providing cover and a natural look.
  • Indoor House Plant: Can be grown indoors in pots or containers, provided the soil remains consistently moist.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Moisture: Thrives in moist conditions, making it suitable for various aquatic and semi-aquatic environments.
  • Lighting: Prefers moderate to high lighting to maintain its vibrant green color and healthy growth.

Benefits:

  • Easy Care: Requires minimal maintenance, making it ideal for beginners and experienced plant enthusiasts.
  • Versatile Planting: Does not need to be rooted, allowing for flexible placement as a floating plant or anchored in substrate.
  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a unique and attractive element to ponds, aquariums, and indoor plant collections with its distinctive leaf shape and trailing growth habit.

Conclusion:

Hydrocotyle verticillata (Whorled Pennywort) is a versatile and attractive plant that can enhance the beauty of garden ponds, aquariums, and indoor plant setups. Its unique trailing growth, ease of care, and ability to thrive in moist conditions make it a popular choice among plant enthusiasts. Whether used as a floating plant, anchored in substrate, or grown indoors, Hydrocotyle verticillata adds a lush, green aesthetic to any environment.

Transform your space with the unique beauty and versatility of Hydrocotyle verticillata, and enjoy the vibrant, natural touch it brings to your aquatic and terrestrial landscapes.
